#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation script to ensure the AIDE file-integrity tool is installed
# on a RHEL 8 system. This script is idempotent: it only installs the
# package if it is not already present.

set -o pipefail

PACKAGE="aide"

# Check if the package is already installed
if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is already installed. No action needed."
    exit 0
fi

echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is not installed. Attempting installation..."

# Prefer dnf, fall back to yum if necessary
if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dnf install -y "${PACKAGE}"
    rc=$?
elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum install -y "${PACKAGE}"
    rc=$?
else
    echo "ERROR: Neither 'dnf' nor 'yum' package manager found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ "${rc}" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to install package '${PACKAGE}'." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Verify installation succeeded
if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' was successfully installed."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Package '${PACKAGE}' installation could not be verified." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Idempotent remediation: ensure AIDE is installed and a valid baseline
# integrity database exists in its active location.
#
set -euo pipefail

AIDE_BIN="/usr/sbin/aide"
AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"
ACTIVE_DB="/var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz"
NEW_DB="/var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz"

# Must run as root
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# 1. Ensure AIDE package is installed
if ! rpm -q aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Installing AIDE package..."
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y aide
    else
        yum install -y aide
    fi
else
    echo "AIDE package already installed."
fi

# Verify the aide binary exists after install
if [[ ! -x "$AIDE_BIN" ]]; then
    if command -v aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        AIDE_BIN="$(command -v aide)"
    else
        echo "ERROR: AIDE binary not found after installation." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# 2. Ensure the active baseline database exists; if not, initialize it.
need_init=0
if [[ ! -s "$ACTIVE_DB" ]]; then
    echo "Active AIDE database not found at $ACTIVE_DB; initialization required."
    need_init=1
else
    # Validate that the existing database is usable/non-corrupt by attempting
    # to read it. If gzip integrity fails, regenerate.
    if ! gzip -t "$ACTIVE_DB" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Existing AIDE database appears corrupt; reinitializing."
        need_init=1
    else
        echo "Valid AIDE baseline database already present at $ACTIVE_DB."
    fi
fi

if [[ "$need_init" -eq 1 ]]; then
    # Ensure database directory exists
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$ACTIVE_DB")"

    echo "Initializing AIDE baseline database (this may take a while)..."
    if [[ -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]]; then
        "$AIDE_BIN" --init --config="$AIDE_CONF"
    else
        "$AIDE_BIN" --init
    fi

    # The init produces the .new database; install it as the active baseline.
    if [[ -s "$NEW_DB" ]]; then
        mv -f "$NEW_DB" "$ACTIVE_DB"
    elif [[ -s "$ACTIVE_DB" ]]; then
        # Some configurations may write directly to the active db
        :
    else
        echo "ERROR: AIDE initialization did not produce a database." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Tighten permissions on the baseline database
    chmod 0600 "$ACTIVE_DB"

    echo "AIDE baseline database installed at $ACTIVE_DB."
fi

# 3. Final verification that an active, readable baseline exists.
if [[ -s "$ACTIVE_DB" ]] && gzip -t "$ACTIVE_DB" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Remediation complete: AIDE is installed and a valid baseline exists."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: AIDE baseline verification failed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

# Audit tools that must be monitored by AIDE
AUDIT_TOOLS=(
    "/usr/sbin/auditctl"
    "/usr/sbin/auditd"
    "/usr/sbin/ausearch"
    "/usr/sbin/aureport"
    "/usr/sbin/autrace"
    "/usr/sbin/augenrules"
)

# AIDE rule covering permissions, ownership, and content hashes
AIDE_RULE="p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512"

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Ensure the configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]; then
    touch "$AIDE_CONF"
fi

# Add/ensure each audit tool entry is present with the proper rule
for tool in "${AUDIT_TOOLS[@]}"; do
    # Escape path for use in regex
    escaped_tool="$(printf '%s' "$tool" | sed 's/[][\.*^$/]/\\&/g')"

    if grep -Eq "^${escaped_tool}[[:space:]]+${AIDE_RULE}[[:space:]]*$" "$AIDE_CONF"; then
        # Entry already correct
        continue
    fi

    if grep -Eq "^${escaped_tool}[[:space:]]+" "$AIDE_CONF"; then
        # Entry exists but with wrong rule, fix it
        sed -i -E "s|^${escaped_tool}[[:space:]]+.*$|${tool} ${AIDE_RULE}|" "$AIDE_CONF"
    else
        # Entry missing, append it
        echo "${tool} ${AIDE_RULE}" >> "$AIDE_CONF"
    fi
done

echo "AIDE configuration updated for audit tools."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure AIDE periodic scan results are emailed to root.
# Idempotent — safe to run multiple times.

set -euo pipefail

CRON_FILE="/etc/crontab"
NOTIFY_EMAIL="root@localhost"

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Installing aide package..."
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Ensure mailx (for the 'mail' command) is available so email delivery works
if ! command -v mail >/dev/null 2>&1 && ! rpm -q mailx >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Installing mailx package..."
    yum install -y mailx
fi

# Initialize the AIDE database if it does not already exist
if [ ! -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz ]; then
    echo "Initializing AIDE database (this may take a while)..."
    /usr/sbin/aide --init
    if [ -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz ]; then
        mv -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
    fi
fi

# The cron job line that runs AIDE and emails the results to root.
# Using a fixed comment marker so we can detect/replace idempotently.
CRON_MARKER="# AIDE periodic integrity check (managed by remediation script)"
CRON_CMD="0 5 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /usr/bin/mail -s \"\$(hostname) - Daily AIDE integrity check run\" ${NOTIFY_EMAIL}"

# Make sure crontab file exists
if [ ! -f "$CRON_FILE" ]; then
    touch "$CRON_FILE"
    chmod 0644 "$CRON_FILE"
fi

# Remove any existing managed AIDE cron entries (marker + following aide line)
# and any stray aide --check cron lines that pipe to mail, to avoid duplicates.
tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
awk -v marker="$CRON_MARKER" '
    $0 == marker { skip_next=1; next }
    skip_next==1 && $0 ~ /aide[[:space:]]+--check/ { skip_next=0; next }
    { skip_next=0; print }
' "$CRON_FILE" > "$tmpfile"

# Also strip any other existing aide --check lines that mail results
grep -v -E '/usr/sbin/aide[[:space:]]+--check.*\| */usr/bin/mail' "$tmpfile" > "${tmpfile}.2" || true
mv -f "${tmpfile}.2" "$tmpfile"

# Append the managed cron entry
{
    echo "$CRON_MARKER"
    echo "$CRON_CMD"
} >> "$tmpfile"

# Install the updated crontab atomically
cat "$tmpfile" > "$CRON_FILE"
chmod 0644 "$CRON_FILE"
chown root:root "$CRON_FILE"
rm -f "$tmpfile"

# Ensure crond is enabled and running so the scheduled job persists across reboots
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^crond\.service'; then
    systemctl enable crond >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl restart crond >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

echo "AIDE periodic check is configured to email results to ${NOTIFY_EMAIL}."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Ensure all AIDE rulesets include ACL verification on RHEL 8.
# Idempotent remediation: adds the "acl" option to every AIDE rule
# definition line that does not already include it.

set -euo pipefail

AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

# Verify AIDE is installed / config exists
if [ ! -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]; then
    echo "AIDE configuration file not found at ${AIDE_CONF}." >&2
    echo "Install AIDE (e.g. 'yum install aide') before running this script." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Make a timestamped backup once per run
backup="${AIDE_CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -p -- "$AIDE_CONF" "$backup"

# Process the config:
# A ruleset definition line has the form:
#   NAME = perm+inode+...+sha256
# We add 'acl' to the option list (right-hand side) if not already present.
#
# We deliberately skip:
#   - comment lines (starting with #)
#   - blank lines
#   - directive lines starting with '@@' (e.g. @@define, @@include)
#   - selection lines that map a path to a rule (start with '/', '!', '=')

tmpfile="$(mktemp)"

awk '
{
    line = $0

    # Strip leading whitespace for testing
    test = line
    sub(/^[[:space:]]+/, "", test)

    # Pass through comments, blanks, directives, and path-selection lines unchanged
    if (test ~ /^#/ || test == "" || test ~ /^@@/ || test ~ /^[\/!=]/) {
        print line
        next
    }

    # A ruleset definition must contain "=" (NAME = options)
    eq = index(line, "=")
    if (eq == 0) {
        print line
        next
    }

    left  = substr(line, 1, eq)        # includes the "="
    right = substr(line, eq + 1)       # the option list

    # Separate any trailing inline comment from the options
    comment = ""
    hash = index(right, "#")
    if (hash > 0) {
        comment = substr(right, hash)
        right   = substr(right, 1, hash - 1)
    }

    # Check whether "acl" already appears as a discrete option in the RHS
    has_acl = 0
    n = split(right, parts, /[+[:space:]]+/)
    for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
        if (parts[i] == "acl") {
            has_acl = 1
            break
        }
    }

    if (has_acl) {
        print line
        next
    }

    # Trim trailing whitespace from option list, then append +acl
    sub(/[[:space:]]+$/, "", right)

    if (right == "") {
        # Malformed/empty RHS; leave untouched
        print line
        next
    }

    newline = left right "+acl"
    if (comment != "") {
        newline = newline " " comment
    }
    print newline
}
' "$AIDE_CONF" > "$tmpfile"

# Only replace if something actually changed
if ! cmp -s "$tmpfile" "$AIDE_CONF"; then
    cat "$tmpfile" > "$AIDE_CONF"
    echo "Updated ${AIDE_CONF}: added 'acl' to AIDE rulesets missing it."
    echo "Backup saved to ${backup}"
else
    echo "No changes needed: all AIDE rulesets already include 'acl'."
    rm -f -- "$backup"
fi

rm -f -- "$tmpfile"

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Ensure AIDE rulesets verify extended attributes (xattrs).
# Idempotent remediation for RHEL 8.
#
set -euo pipefail

AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

# Make sure AIDE is installed and the config exists.
if ! rpm -q aide >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed; installing..."
    yum install -y aide
fi

if [[ ! -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: AIDE configuration file $AIDE_CONF not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Backup once (only if a backup doesn't already exist for today's run state).
backup="${AIDE_CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
cp -a "$AIDE_CONF" "$backup"

# A ruleset definition line looks like:
#   NAME = p+i+n+u+g+s+...
# We want to ensure 'xattrs' is present in the attribute list (right-hand side).
#
# Identify candidate definition lines:
#   - not comments (do not start with optional whitespace then '#')
#   - not '@@' directives (macros/includes like @@define, @@include)
#   - contain an '=' sign
#   - left-hand side is a single token (a ruleset/group name), no spaces
#   - right-hand side contains '+'-separated attributes or known attribute tokens
#
# For matching lines that don't already contain 'xattrs', append '+xattrs'.

tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$tmpfile"' EXIT

awk '
{
    line = $0

    # Preserve comments and directives unchanged
    stripped = line
    sub(/^[ \t]+/, "", stripped)
    if (stripped ~ /^#/ || stripped ~ /^@@/ || stripped == "") {
        print line
        next
    }

    # Must contain an = sign
    eq = index(line, "=")

# We want to ensure 'xattrs' is present in the attribute list (right-hand side).
#

#   - not '@@' directives (macros/includes like @@define, @@include)

#   - right-hand side contains '+'-separated attributes or known attribute tokens

tmpfile="$(mktemp)"

    # Only treat as a ruleset if rhs looks like an attribute expression:

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Set the system-wide cryptographic policy to DEFAULT on RHEL 8.
# This ensures all applications/services use vetted, non-weak ciphers/algorithms.
# The script is idempotent and persists the policy across reboots.

set -o nounset
set -o pipefail

DESIRED_POLICY="DEFAULT"
CONFIG_FILE="/etc/crypto-policies/config"
UPDATE_CMD="/usr/bin/update-crypto-policies"

# Must run as root to apply the policy.
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the crypto-policies tooling is present.
if [[ ! -x "${UPDATE_CMD}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${UPDATE_CMD} not found. Installing crypto-policies-scripts..." >&2
    if command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y crypto-policies-scripts || {
            echo "ERROR: Failed to install crypto-policies-scripts." >&2
            exit 1
        }
    else
        echo "ERROR: Cannot install required package; yum not available." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Determine the currently active policy.
current_policy="$(${UPDATE_CMD} --show 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]')"

needs_apply=0

if [[ "${current_policy}" != "${DESIRED_POLICY}" ]]; then
    echo "Current crypto policy is '${current_policy:-unknown}'; expected '${DESIRED_POLICY}'."
    needs_apply=1
fi

# Verify the config back-end file reflects the desired policy.
if [[ -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
    file_policy="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*[^#[:space:]]' "${CONFIG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | tr -d '[:space:]')"
    if [[ "${file_policy}" != "${DESIRED_POLICY}" ]]; then
        echo "Config back-end '${CONFIG_FILE}' shows '${file_policy:-empty}'; expected '${DESIRED_POLICY}'."
        needs_apply=1
    fi
else
    echo "Config back-end '${CONFIG_FILE}' missing."
    needs_apply=1
fi

if [[ "${needs_apply}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo "Applying system-wide crypto policy: ${DESIRED_POLICY}"
    if ! "${UPDATE_CMD}" --set "${DESIRED_POLICY}"; then
        echo "ERROR: Failed to set crypto policy to ${DESIRED_POLICY}." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
else
    echo "System-wide crypto policy already set to ${DESIRED_POLICY}; no changes needed."
fi

# Final verification to confirm persistence and back-end consistency.
final_policy="$(${UPDATE_CMD} --show 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]')"
final_file_policy=""
if [[ -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
    final_file_policy="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*[^#[:space:]]' "${CONFIG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | tr -d '[:space:]')"
fi

if [[ "${final_policy}" == "${DESIRED_POLICY}" && "${final_file_policy}" == "${DESIRED_POLICY}" ]]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: System-wide cryptographic policy is '${DESIRED_POLICY}' and persisted in ${CONFIG_FILE}."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Verification failed. Active='${final_policy}', Config='${final_file_policy}'." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure GnuTLS honors the system-wide crypto policy and does not
# permit legacy/insecure protocol versions (SSL3.0, TLS1.0, TLS1.1, DTLS0.9, DTLS1.0).
#
set -u

GNUTLS_CONF="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/gnutls.config"
GNUTLS_DIR="$(dirname "$GNUTLS_CONF")"

# The required disabled protocol versions.
REQUIRED_DISABLED="-VERS-SSL3.0:-VERS-TLS1.0:-VERS-TLS1.1:-VERS-DTLS0.9:-VERS-DTLS1.0"

# Ensure the back-ends directory exists.
if [ ! -d "$GNUTLS_DIR" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$GNUTLS_DIR"
fi

# Prefer regenerating the system-wide crypto policy so the GnuTLS back-end is
# managed centrally. Use the current policy if set, otherwise default to DEFAULT.
if command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_POLICY="$(update-crypto-policies --show 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]')"
    if [ -z "$CURRENT_POLICY" ]; then
        CURRENT_POLICY="DEFAULT"
    fi
    update-crypto-policies --set "$CURRENT_POLICY" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# Determine whether the GnuTLS back-end is a symlink managed by crypto-policies.
# If it is a managed symlink and already disables the required protocols, we are done.
needs_fix=0

if [ -e "$GNUTLS_CONF" ] || [ -L "$GNUTLS_CONF" ]; then
    # Read effective content (follows symlink).
    if grep -Eq -- '-VERS-SSL3\.0' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.0'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.1'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-DTLS0\.9' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-DTLS1\.0' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
        needs_fix=0
    else
        needs_fix=1
    fi
else
    needs_fix=1
fi

if [ "$needs_fix" -eq 1 ]; then
    # If it's a symlink (managed by crypto-policies) but missing the directives,
    # the crypto-policy itself is too weak. Re-point to DEFAULT to ensure compliance.
    if [ -L "$GNUTLS_CONF" ] && command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi

    # Re-evaluate after potential policy reset.
    if grep -Eq -- '-VERS-SSL3\.0' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.0'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.1'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-DTLS0\.9' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-DTLS1\.0' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
        needs_fix=0
    fi
fi

if [ "$needs_fix" -eq 1 ]; then
    # As a fallback, write an explicit, compliant GnuTLS back-end config.
    # Replace any existing symlink with a regular file.
    if [ -L "$GNUTLS_CONF" ]; then
        rm -f "$GNUTLS_CONF"
    fi

    tmpfile="$(mktemp "${GNUTLS_DIR}/.gnutls.config.XXXXXX")"
    cat > "$tmpfile" <<EOF
[overrides]
disabled-version = ${REQUIRED_DISABLED}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure GnuTLS honors the system-wide crypto policy and does not
# permit legacy/insecure protocol versions (SSL3.0, TLS1.0, TLS1.1, DTLS0.9, DTLS1.0).
#
set -u

GNUTLS_CONF="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/gnutls.config"
GNUTLS_DIR="$(dirname "$GNUTLS_CONF")"

# The required disabled protocol versions.
REQUIRED_DISABLED="-VERS-SSL3.0:-VERS-TLS1.0:-VERS-TLS1.1:-VERS-DTLS0.9:-VERS-DTLS1.0"

# Ensure the back-ends directory exists.
if [ ! -d "$GNUTLS_DIR" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$GNUTLS_DIR"
fi

# Prefer regenerating the system-wide crypto policy so the GnuTLS back-end is
# managed centrally. Use the current policy if set, otherwise default to DEFAULT.
if command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CURRENT_POLICY="$(update-crypto-policies --show 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]')"
    if [ -z "$CURRENT_POLICY" ]; then
        CURRENT_POLICY="DEFAULT"
    fi
    update-crypto-policies --set "$CURRENT_POLICY" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# Determine whether the GnuTLS back-end is a symlink managed by crypto-policies.
# If it is a managed symlink and already disables the required protocols, we are done.
needs_fix=0

if [ -e "$GNUTLS_CONF" ] || [ -L "$GNUTLS_CONF" ]; then
    # Read effective content (follows symlink).
    if grep -Eq -- '-VERS-SSL3\.0' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.0'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.1'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-DTLS0\.9' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-DTLS1\.0' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
        needs_fix=0
    else
        needs_fix=1
    fi
else
    needs_fix=1
fi

if [ "$needs_fix" -eq 1 ]; then
    # If it's a symlink (managed by crypto-policies) but missing the directives,
    # the crypto-policy itself is too weak. Re-point to DEFAULT to ensure compliance.
    if [ -L "$GNUTLS_CONF" ] && command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi

    # Re-evaluate after potential policy reset.
    if grep -Eq -- '-VERS-SSL3\.0' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.0'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.1'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-DTLS0\.9' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
       grep -Eq -- '-VERS-DTLS1\.0' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
        needs_fix=0
    fi
fi

if [ "$needs_fix" -eq 1 ]; then
    # As a fallback, write an explicit, compliant GnuTLS back-end config.
    # Replace any existing symlink with a regular file.
    if [ -L "$GNUTLS_CONF" ]; then
        rm -f "$GNUTLS_CONF"
    fi

    tmpfile="$(mktemp "${GNUTLS_DIR}/.gnutls.config.XXXXXX")"
    cat > "$tmpfile" <<EOF

disabled-version = ${REQUIRED_DISABLED}
EOF

    chown root:root "$tmpfile" 2>/dev/null || true
    mv -f "$tmpfile" "$GNUTLS_CONF"
fi

if grep -Eq -- '-VERS-SSL3\.0' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
   grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.0'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
   grep -Eq -- '-VERS-TLS1\.1'  "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \
   grep -Eq -- '-VERS-DTLS0\.9' "$GNUTLS_CONF" 2>/dev/null && \

    echo "GnuTLS back-end is compliant: legacy/insecure protocols are disabled."
    exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#

# system-wide cryptographic policy (FIPS) rather than a local override.

set -o pipefail

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CRYPTO_BACKEND="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config"
SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"

# FIPS 140-2 validated ciphers, ordered strongest-to-weakest

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Sanity checks
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if ! command -v update-crypto-policies >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: update-crypto-policies not found; crypto-policies package required." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Ensure the system-wide crypto policy is set to FIPS so that the
#    back-end is regenerated with FIPS-validated algorithms.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CURRENT_POLICY="$(update-crypto-policies --show 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')"
if [[ "${CURRENT_POLICY}" != "FIPS" ]]; then
    echo "Setting system-wide crypto policy to FIPS (current: ${CURRENT_POLICY:-unknown})..."
    update-crypto-policies --set FIPS >/dev/null 2>&1
    if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: Failed to set crypto policy to FIPS." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
else
    echo "System-wide crypto policy already set to FIPS."
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Remove any local override of Ciphers in sshd_config and drop-in files,
#    so the SSH server falls back to the system-wide crypto policy.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
remove_cipher_override() {
    local file="$1"
    [[ -f "${file}" ]] || return 0
    if grep -Eiq '^[[:space:]]*Ciphers[[:space:]]' "${file}"; then
        echo "Removing local 'Ciphers' override from ${file}..."
        sed -ri '/^[[:space:]]*Ciphers[[:space:]]/d' "${file}"
    fi
}

remove_cipher_override "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

if [[ -d "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" ]]; then
    shopt -s nullglob
    for f in "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}"/*.conf; do
        remove_cipher_override "${f}"
    done
    shopt -u nullglob
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Ensure the crypto-policy back-end contains the correct, uncommented
#    cipher specification consistent with the system policy.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CIPHER_LINE="CIPHERS=\"${CIPHERS}\""

ensure_backend() {
    local file="$1"
    local tmp
    tmp="$(mktemp)" || { echo "ERROR: mktemp failed." >&2; exit 1; }

    if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
        # Drop any existing (commented or uncommented) CIPHERS lines to avoid
        # fragmentation, then re-add a single canonical entry.
        grep -Ev '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*CIPHERS=' "${file}" > "${tmp}"
    else
        : > "${tmp}"
    fi

    echo "${CIPHER_LINE}" >> "${tmp}"

    # Only replace if content actually differs (idempotency)
    if [[ -f "${file}" ]] && cmp -s "${tmp}" "${file}"; then
        echo "Crypto-policy back-end ${file} already correct."
        rm -f "${tmp}"
        return 0
    fi

    install -m 0644 -o root -g root "${tmp}" "${file}"
    rm -f "${tmp}"
    echo "Updated crypto-policy back-end ${file}."
}

# The back-end is a symlink managed by crypto-policies; resolve real target.
if [[ -L "${CRYPTO_BACKEND}" ]]; then
    REAL_BACKEND="$(readlink -f "${CRYPTO_BACKEND}")"
else
    REAL_BACKEND="${CRYPTO_BACKEND}"
fi

ensure_backend "${REAL_BACKEND}"

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Validate sshd configuration and reload the service if running.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "ERROR: sshd configuration test failed; not reloading." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Reloading sshd to apply configuration..."
        systemctl reload sshd 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart sshd
    fi
fi

echo "Remediation complete: SSH server uses system-wide FIPS crypto policy for ciphers."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure OpenSSH server MAC algorithms are governed by the
# system-wide crypto policy back-end (no local override in sshd_config).
#
# A "MACs" directive in sshd_config (or its drop-in files) overrides the
# crypto-policy back-end referenced via the CRYPTO_POLICY include. Removing
# such overrides re-delegates MAC selection to the system-wide policy.

set -o nounset
set -o pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"

changed=0

# Backup helper (only once per file, only when a change is actually made)
backup_file() {
    local f="$1"
    if [ -f "$f" ] && [ ! -f "${f}.scc-bak" ]; then
        cp -p -- "$f" "${f}.scc-bak"
    fi
}

# Comment out any active (non-commented) MACs directive in a given file.
remove_macs_override() {
    local f="$1"
    [ -f "$f" ] || return 0

    # Detect an active MACs line (case-insensitive keyword, leading whitespace ok)
    if grep -Eiq '^[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]' "$f"; then
        backup_file "$f"
        # Comment out the offending line(s) idempotently.
        sed -ri 's/^([[:space:]]*)(MACs[[:space:]].*)$/\1# \2 # removed: governed by system crypto policy/I' "$f"
        echo "Removed local MACs override from: $f"
        changed=1
    fi
}

# Process main sshd_config
remove_macs_override "$SSHD_CONFIG"

# Process drop-in files
if [ -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ]; then
    shopt -s nullglob
    for f in "$DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf; do
        remove_macs_override "$f"
    done
    shopt -u nullglob
fi

# Ensure the crypto-policy back-end is included so sshd honors system policy.
# RHEL 8 ships this include by default; restore it if missing.
CRYPTO_INCLUDE_LINE="Include /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config"
LEGACY_INCLUDE_PATTERN='^[[:space:]]*Include[[:space:]]+/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver\.config'

if [ -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    if ! grep -Eq "$LEGACY_INCLUDE_PATTERN" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        # On RHEL 8 the crypto policy back-end is normally pulled in via the
        # OPTIONS/CRYPTO_POLICY mechanism in /etc/sysconfig/sshd, but if no
        # include is present we add one to centralize crypto config.
        backup_file "$SSHD_CONFIG"
        printf '\n# Delegate crypto (incl. MACs) to system-wide crypto policy\n%s\n' \
            "$CRYPTO_INCLUDE_LINE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
        echo "Added crypto-policy back-end include to: $SSHD_CONFIG"
        changed=1
    fi
fi

# Ensure /etc/sysconfig/sshd does not disable the crypto policy hook.
SYSCONFIG_SSHD="/etc/sysconfig/sshd"
if [ -f "$SYSCONFIG_SSHD" ]; then
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*CRYPTO_POLICY=' "$SYSCONFIG_SSHD"; then
        backup_file "$SYSCONFIG_SSHD"
        sed -ri 's/^([[:space:]]*CRYPTO_POLICY=.*)$/# \1 # removed: do not bypass system crypto policy/' "$SYSCONFIG_SSHD"
        echo "Re-enabled system crypto policy hook in: $SYSCONFIG_SSHD"
        changed=1
    fi
fi

# Validate resulting sshd configuration before attempting a reload.
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "WARNING: sshd configuration test failed after remediation." >&2
        echo "Review ${SSHD_CONFIG} and ${DROPIN_DIR}; backups end in .scc-bak" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Reload sshd only if something changed and the service is active.
if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd 2>/dev/null; then
            systemctl reload sshd 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart sshd 2>/dev/null || true
            echo "Reloaded sshd to apply system crypto policy."
        fi
    fi
    echo "Remediation complete: SSH MAC selection now governed by system crypto policy."
else
    echo "System already compliant: SSH MAC selection governed by system crypto policy."
fi

exit 0

set -euo pipefail

PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"

SETTINGS_FILE="${DB_DIR}/00-login-screen"
LOCKS_DIR="${DB_DIR}/locks"
LOCKS_FILE="${LOCKS_DIR}/00-login-screen"

mkdir -p "${PROFILE_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${LOCKS_DIR}"

user-db:user

file-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults
EOF
else
    if ! grep -qx "user-db:user" "${PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        printf 'user-db:user\n' >> "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
    if ! grep -qx "system-db:gdm" "${PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        printf 'system-db:gdm\n' >> "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi

cat > "${SETTINGS_FILE}" <<'EOF'
[org/gnome/login-screen]
disable-user-list=true
EOF

# Lock the setting so users cannot override it
cat > "${LOCKS_FILE}" <<'EOF'
/org/gnome/login-screen/disable-user-list
EOF

# Update the dconf databases
dconf update

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

GDM_CONF="/etc/gdm/custom.conf"

# If GDM is not installed, there is nothing to remediate.
if ! rpm -q gdm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "GDM is not installed; no remediation needed."
    exit 0
fi

# Ensure the GDM configuration file exists.
if [ ! -f "$GDM_CONF" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$GDM_CONF")"
    touch "$GDM_CONF"
    chmod 0644 "$GDM_CONF"
fi

# Ensure the [daemon] section exists.
if ! grep -qE '^\s*\[daemon\]\s*$' "$GDM_CONF"; then
    printf '\n[daemon]\n' >> "$GDM_CONF"
fi

# Remediate AutomaticLoginEnable within the [daemon] section.
if grep -qE '^\s*AutomaticLoginEnable\s*=' "$GDM_CONF"; then
    # Replace any existing value with false.
    sed -ri 's|^\s*AutomaticLoginEnable\s*=.*|AutomaticLoginEnable=false|' "$GDM_CONF"
else
    # Insert AutomaticLoginEnable=false right after the [daemon] header.
    sed -ri '/^\s*\[daemon\]\s*$/a AutomaticLoginEnable=false' "$GDM_CONF"
fi

# Also disable TimedLoginEnable to prevent timed automatic login.
if grep -qE '^\s*TimedLoginEnable\s*=' "$GDM_CONF"; then
    sed -ri 's|^\s*TimedLoginEnable\s*=.*|TimedLoginEnable=false|' "$GDM_CONF"
else
    sed -ri '/^\s*\[daemon\]\s*$/a TimedLoginEnable=false' "$GDM_CONF"
fi

echo "Automatic graphical login has been disabled in $GDM_CONF."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Enforce a system-wide, locked 15-minute (900 second) GNOME
# screensaver/idle activation timeout so individual users cannot override it.
#
set -euo pipefail

TIMEOUT_SECONDS=900

DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCK_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE="/etc/dconf/profile/user"

SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}/00-screensaver"

# Ensure required tooling is present.
if ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "dconf is not installed; installing dconf..."
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y dconf
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y dconf
    else
        echo "ERROR: Unable to install dconf (no dnf/yum found)." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Create required directories.
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCK_DIR}"
mkdir -p "$(dirname "${DCONF_PROFILE}")"

# Ensure the dconf user profile references the local database.
if [ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE}" ] || \
   ! grep -qxF "user-db:user" "${DCONF_PROFILE}" 2>/dev/null || \
   ! grep -qxF "system-db:local" "${DCONF_PROFILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE}" <<'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
fi

# Write the screensaver/idle settings.
DESIRED_SETTINGS=$(cat <<EOF
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 ${TIMEOUT_SECONDS}

[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
idle-activation-enabled=true
lock-enabled=true
lock-delay=uint32 0
EOF
)

if [ ! -f "${SETTINGS_FILE}" ] || \
   [ "$(cat "${SETTINGS_FILE}")" != "${DESIRED_SETTINGS}" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_SETTINGS}" > "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
fi

# Lock the settings so users cannot override them.
DESIRED_LOCKS=$(cat <<'EOF'
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/idle-activation-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
EOF
)

if [ ! -f "${LOCKS_FILE}" ] || \
   [ "$(cat "${LOCKS_FILE}")" != "${DESIRED_LOCKS}" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_LOCKS}" > "${LOCKS_FILE}"
fi

# Update the dconf system databases.
dconf update

echo "GNOME idle timeout enforced and locked at ${TIMEOUT_SECONDS} seconds."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
PROFILE_FILE="${PROFILE_DIR}/user"
DB_NAME="local"
KEYFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/${DB_NAME}.d"
LOCKS_DIR="${KEYFILE_DIR}/locks"
SETTINGS_FILE="${KEYFILE_DIR}/00-security-settings"
LOCK_FILE="${LOCKS_DIR}/00-security-settings-lock"
IDLE_DELAY_VALUE="900"

mkdir -p "${PROFILE_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${KEYFILE_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${LOCKS_DIR}"

# Ensure the dconf user profile references the system database.
if [ ! -f "${PROFILE_FILE}" ]; then
    cat > "${PROFILE_FILE}" <<EOF
user-db:user
system-db:${DB_NAME}
EOF
else
    if ! grep -qx "user-db:user" "${PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        sed -i '1i user-db:user' "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
    if ! grep -qx "system-db:${DB_NAME}" "${PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        echo "system-db:${DB_NAME}" >> "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# Set the system-wide idle-delay value in the local database keyfile.
if [ -f "${SETTINGS_FILE}" ] && grep -q '^\[org/gnome/desktop/session\]' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
    if grep -q '^[[:space:]]*idle-delay[[:space:]]*=' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
        sed -i "s|^[[:space:]]*idle-delay[[:space:]]*=.*|idle-delay=uint32 ${IDLE_DELAY_VALUE}|" "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
    else
        sed -i "/^\[org\/gnome\/desktop\/session\]/a idle-delay=uint32 ${IDLE_DELAY_VALUE}" "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
    fi
else
    cat >> "${SETTINGS_FILE}" <<EOF
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 ${IDLE_DELAY_VALUE}
EOF
fi

# Lock the idle-delay key so users cannot override it.
if [ -f "${LOCK_FILE}" ]; then
    if ! grep -qx "/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay" "${LOCK_FILE}"; then
        echo "/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay" >> "${LOCK_FILE}"
    fi
else
    cat > "${LOCK_FILE}" <<EOF
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
PROFILE_FILE="${PROFILE_DIR}/user"
DB_NAME="local"
KEYFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/${DB_NAME}.d"
LOCKS_DIR="${KEYFILE_DIR}/locks"
SETTINGS_FILE="${KEYFILE_DIR}/00-security-settings"
LOCK_FILE="${LOCKS_DIR}/00-security-settings-lock"
IDLE_DELAY_VALUE="900"

mkdir -p "${PROFILE_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${KEYFILE_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${LOCKS_DIR}"

# Ensure the dconf user profile references the system database.
if [ ! -f "${PROFILE_FILE}" ]; then
    cat > "${PROFILE_FILE}" <<EOF
user-db:user
system-db:${DB_NAME}
EOF
else
    if ! grep -qx "user-db:user" "${PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        sed -i '1i user-db:user' "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
    if ! grep -qx "system-db:${DB_NAME}" "${PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        echo "system-db:${DB_NAME}" >> "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# Set the system-wide idle-delay value in the local database keyfile.
if [ -f "${SETTINGS_FILE}" ] && grep -q '^\[org/gnome/desktop/session\]' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
    if grep -q '^[[:space:]]*idle-delay[[:space:]]*=' "${SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
        sed -i "s|^[[:space:]]*idle-delay[[:space:]]*=.*|idle-delay=uint32 ${IDLE_DELAY_VALUE}|" "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
    else
        sed -i "/^\[org\/gnome\/desktop\/session\]/a idle-delay=uint32 ${IDLE_DELAY_VALUE}" "${SETTINGS_FILE}"
    fi
else
    cat >> "${SETTINGS_FILE}" <<EOF
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 ${IDLE_DELAY_VALUE}
EOF
fi

# Lock the idle-delay key so users cannot override it.
if [ -f "${LOCK_FILE}" ]; then
    if ! grep -qx "/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay" "${LOCK_FILE}"; then
        echo "/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay" >> "${LOCK_FILE}"
    fi
else
    cat > "${LOCK_FILE}" <<EOF
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
EOF
fi

# Update the dconf system databases.
dconf update

echo "Session idle-delay enforced and locked at ${IDLE_DELAY_VALUE} seconds."

TIMEOUT_MINUTES=5

    echo "ERROR: overall sudoers validation failed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure sudo requires the invoking user's OWN password.
#
# This enforces:
#   Defaults !rootpw     - do NOT prompt for the root account's password
#   Defaults !targetpw   - do NOT prompt for the target user's password
#   Defaults !runaspw    - do NOT prompt for the run-as user's password
#
# The net effect: sudo validates only the invoking user's credentials.
#
set -euo pipefail

SUDOERS_DROPIN="/etc/sudoers.d/zz_disable_alt_pw_prompts"
MAIN_SUDOERS="/etc/sudoers"

# The directives we want enforced.
declare -A DIRECTIVES=(
    ["rootpw"]="Defaults !rootpw"
    ["targetpw"]="Defaults !targetpw"
    ["runaspw"]="Defaults !runaspw"
)

# Sanity check: visudo must exist for safe validation.
if ! command -v visudo >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: visudo not found; cannot safely manage sudoers." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# 1) Remove/neutralize any conflicting positive enablements in /etc/sudoers
#    (e.g. "Defaults rootpw", "Defaults targetpw", "Defaults runaspw")
#    We comment out lines that ENABLE these (without the leading '!').
if [[ -f "$MAIN_SUDOERS" ]]; then
    cp -a "$MAIN_SUDOERS" "${MAIN_SUDOERS}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
    for key in "${!DIRECTIVES[@]}"; do
        # Comment out lines enabling the directive (no '!' before the key).
        # Matches: Defaults<sep>...rootpw   but NOT Defaults !rootpw
        sed -ri "s/^([[:space:]]*Defaults[[:space:]]+[^#!]*\b${key}\b)/# DISABLED by remediation: \1/g" "$MAIN_SUDOERS"
    done
fi

# 2) Remove conflicting positive enablements in any /etc/sudoers.d/* files
#    except our own drop-in.
if [[ -d /etc/sudoers.d ]]; then
    for f in /etc/sudoers.d/*; do
        [[ -e "$f" ]] || continue
        [[ "$f" == "$SUDOERS_DROPIN" ]] && continue
        # Skip backups we might create
        case "$f" in
            *.bak*) continue ;;
        esac
        changed=0
        cp -a "$f" "${f}.preremediate" 2>/dev/null || true
        for key in "${!DIRECTIVES[@]}"; do
            if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*Defaults[[:space:]]+[^#!]*\b${key}\b" "$f"; then
                sed -ri "s/^([[:space:]]*Defaults[[:space:]]+[^#!]*\b${key}\b)/# DISABLED by remediation: \1/g" "$f"
                changed=1
            fi
        done
        if [[ "$changed" -eq 0 ]]; then
            rm -f "${f}.preremediate" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    done
fi

# 3) Write our authoritative drop-in enforcing the disabled prompts.
TMP_DROPIN="$(mktemp)"
cat > "$TMP_DROPIN" <<'EOF'
# Managed file: enforce that sudo authenticates the INVOKING user only.
# Explicitly disable prompting for root, target, or run-as passwords.
Defaults !rootpw
Defaults !targetpw
Defaults !runaspw
EOF

chmod 0440 "$TMP_DROPIN"

# 4) Validate the resulting configuration before installing.
#    Validate the drop-in itself.
if ! visudo -cf "$TMP_DROPIN" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: generated sudoers drop-in failed validation." >&2
    rm -f "$TMP_DROPIN"
    exit 1
fi

# Install drop-in atomically only if content differs (idempotency).
if [[ -f "$SUDOERS_DROPIN" ]] && cmp -s "$TMP_DROPIN" "$SUDOERS_DROPIN"; then
    rm -f "$TMP_DROPIN"
else
    install -m 0440 -o root -g root "$TMP_DROPIN" "$SUDOERS_DROPIN"
    rm -f "$TMP_DROPIN"
fi

# 5) Final full validation of the entire sudoers configuration.
if ! visudo -cf "$MAIN_SUDOERS" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: overall sudoers validation failed after remediation." >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Remediation complete: sudo now requires the invoking user's own password."
echo "Enforced directives in $SUDOERS_DROPIN:"
echo "  Defaults !rootpw"
echo "  Defaults !targetpw"
echo "  Defaults !runaspw"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure the gssproxy package is absent from the system.
# This script is idempotent: it only acts if the package is present.

set -o pipefail

PACKAGE="gssproxy"

# Must be run as root to remove packages
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Check whether the package is installed
if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is present. Removing..."
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf -y remove "${PACKAGE}"
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum -y remove "${PACKAGE}"
    else
        rpm -e "${PACKAGE}"
    fi

    # Verify removal succeeded
    if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "ERROR: Failed to remove package '${PACKAGE}'." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' has been removed."
else
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is not installed. No action required."
fi

exit 0

#
# Remediation: Ensure the Red Hat Support reporting plugin component of the
# ABRT (Automatic Bug Reporting Tool) framework is not installed.
#
# The package "libreport-plugin-rhtsupport" provides the plugin that submits
# crash reports into the Red Hat Support / Red Hat Technical Support system.
# Removing it reduces attack surface and prevents inadvertent disclosure of
# system information.
#
# This script is idempotent: running it multiple times yields the same result.

set -o nounset
set -o pipefail

PACKAGE="libreport-plugin-rhtsupport"

# Determine an available package manager.
if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    PKG_MGR="dnf"
elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    PKG_MGR="yum"
elif command -v rpm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    PKG_MGR="rpm-only"
else
    echo "ERROR: No supported package manager (dnf/yum/rpm) found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the package is currently installed.
if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is installed. Removing..."
    case "${PKG_MGR}" in
        dnf)
            dnf -y remove "${PACKAGE}"
            ;;
        yum)
            yum -y remove "${PACKAGE}"
            ;;
        rpm-only)
            rpm -e "${PACKAGE}"
            ;;
    esac

    # Verify removal.
    if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "ERROR: Failed to remove package '${PACKAGE}'." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' successfully removed."
else
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is not installed. No action required."
fi

exit 0

    case "${PKG_MGR}" in

            ;;
        yum)

            ;;
        rpm-only)
            rpm -e "${PACKAGE}"
            ;;
    esac

    # Verify removal.
    if rpm -q "${PACKAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "ERROR: Failed to remove package '${PACKAGE}'." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' successfully removed."
else
    echo "Package '${PACKAGE}' is not installed. No action required."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure system authentication/identity is managed by authselect
# using a tested, supported profile (sssd) so the PAM stack and nsswitch
# configuration are not hand-edited. Idempotent and persists across reboots.

set -u

PROFILE="sssd"
LOGTAG="authselect-remediation"

log() {
    echo "[${LOGTAG}] $*"
}

# Ensure authselect is available
if ! command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "authselect not found; attempting to install."
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf -y install authselect || { log "Failed to install authselect."; exit 1; }
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum -y install authselect || { log "Failed to install authselect."; exit 1; }
    else
        log "No supported package manager found to install authselect."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Confirm the desired profile is available/supported on this system
if ! authselect list 2>/dev/null | grep -qw "${PROFILE}"; then
    log "Requested profile '${PROFILE}' is not available."
    exit 1
fi

# Determine the currently active profile (if any)
CURRENT_OUTPUT="$(authselect current 2>/dev/null)"
CURRENT_PROFILE=""
if echo "${CURRENT_OUTPUT}" | grep -q "Profile ID:"; then
    CURRENT_PROFILE="$(echo "${CURRENT_OUTPUT}" | awk -F'Profile ID:' '/Profile ID:/{print $2}' | tr -d ' ')"
fi

# Idempotency check: already configured with the correct profile and valid
if [ "${CURRENT_PROFILE}" = "${PROFILE}" ]; then
    log "Profile '${PROFILE}' already active. Verifying integrity."
    if authselect check >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "authselect configuration is consistent. No changes needed."
        exit 0
    else
        log "Configuration drift detected; reapplying profile '${PROFILE}'."
        if authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
           authselect select "${PROFILE}" with-sudo with-faillock without-nullok --force >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log "Profile '${PROFILE}' reapplied successfully."
            exit 0
        else
            log "Failed to reapply profile '${PROFILE}'."
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
fi

# Select and activate the supported profile (force to overwrite manual edits)
log "Activating supported authentication profile '${PROFILE}'."
if authselect select "${PROFILE}" with-sudo with-faillock without-nullok --force >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "Profile '${PROFILE}' selected and activated."
else
    log "Failed to select profile '${PROFILE}'."
    exit 1
fi

# Verify the resulting configuration
if authselect check >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "authselect configuration verified as consistent."
else
    log "authselect reports an inconsistent configuration after applying."
    exit 1
fi

# Final confirmation
NEW_PROFILE="$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | awk -F'Profile ID:' '/Profile ID:/{print $2}' | tr -d ' ')"
if [ "${NEW_PROFILE}" = "${PROFILE}" ]; then
    log "Remediation complete. Active profile: ${NEW_PROFILE} (persists across reboots)."
    exit 0
else
    log "Remediation failed: active profile is '${NEW_PROFILE}', expected '${PROFILE}'."
    exit 1
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Approved organizational banner text.
# Adjust BANNER_TEXT to exactly match your environment's approved message.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
BANNER_TEXT='You are accessing a U.S. Government (USG) Information System (IS) that is provided for USG-authorized use only. By using this IS (which includes any device attached to this IS), you consent to the following conditions: -The USG routinely intercepts and monitors communications on this IS for purposes including, but not limited to, penetration testing, COMSEC monitoring, network operations and defense, personnel misconduct (PM), law enforcement (LE), and counterintelligence (CI) investigations. -At any time, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS. -Communications using, or data stored on, this IS are not private, are subject to routine monitoring, interception, and search, and may be disclosed or used for any USG-authorized purpose. -This IS includes security measures (e.g., authentication and access controls) to protect USG interests--not for your personal benefit or privacy. -Notwithstanding the above, using this IS does not constitute consent to PM, LE or CI investigative searching or monitoring of the content of privileged communications, or work product, related to personal representation or services by attorneys, psychotherapists, or clergy, and their assistants. Such communications and work product are private and confidential. See User Agreement for details.'

PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
PROFILE_FILE="${PROFILE_DIR}/gdm"
DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/gdm.d"
KEYFILE="${DB_DIR}/01-banner-message"
LOCKS_DIR="${DB_DIR}/locks"
LOCKFILE="${LOCKS_DIR}/01-banner-message"

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ensure dconf tooling is available.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if ! command -v dconf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Installing dconf (required for GDM banner configuration)..." >&2
    yum install -y dconf >/dev/null 2>&1 || dnf install -y dconf >/dev/null 2>&1
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Create required directories.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
install -d -m 0755 "${PROFILE_DIR}"
install -d -m 0755 "${DB_DIR}"
install -d -m 0755 "${LOCKS_DIR}"

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ensure the gdm dconf profile references the gdm database.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROFILE_CONTENT=$'user-db:user\nsystem-db:gdm\nfile-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults'
if [[ ! -f "${PROFILE_FILE}" ]] || [[ "$(cat "${PROFILE_FILE}")" != "${PROFILE_CONTENT}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${PROFILE_CONTENT}" > "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${PROFILE_FILE}"
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Escape banner text for dconf single-quoted GVariant string.
# Backslashes and single quotes must be escaped.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
escape_dconf() {
    local s="$1"
    s="${s//\\/\\\\}"   # escape backslashes
    s="${s//\'/\\\'}"   # escape single quotes
    printf '%s' "$s"
}
ESCAPED_BANNER="$(escape_dconf "${BANNER_TEXT}")"

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Write the keyfile that enables and sets the banner message.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
KEYFILE_CONTENT="[org/gnome/login-screen]
banner-message-enable=true
banner-message-text='${ESCAPED_BANNER}'"

if [[ ! -f "${KEYFILE}" ]] || [[ "$(cat "${KEYFILE}")" != "${KEYFILE_CONTENT}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${KEYFILE_CONTENT}" > "${KEYFILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${KEYFILE}"
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Lock the keys so non-privileged users cannot override them.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
LOCK_CONTENT="/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-enable
/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text"

if [[ ! -f "${LOCKFILE}" ]] || [[ "$(cat "${LOCKFILE}")" != "${LOCK_CONTENT}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${LOCK_CONTENT}" > "${LOCKFILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${LOCKFILE}"
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compile the dconf databases so the changes take effect and persist.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
dconf update

echo "GDM graphical login warning banner configured and locked successfully."

# Ensure the gdm dconf profile references the gdm database.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROFILE_CONTENT=$'user-db:user\nsystem-db:gdm\nfile-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults'
if [[ ! -f "${PROFILE_FILE}" ]] || [[ "$(cat "${PROFILE_FILE}")" != "${PROFILE_CONTENT}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${PROFILE_CONTENT}" > "${PROFILE_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${PROFILE_FILE}"
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Escape banner text for dconf single-quoted GVariant string.
# Backslashes and single quotes must be escaped.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
escape_dconf() {
    local s="$1"
    s="${s//\\/\\\\}"   # escape backslashes
    s="${s//\'/\\\'}"   # escape single quotes
    printf '%s' "$s"
}
ESCAPED_BANNER="$(escape_dconf "${BANNER_TEXT}")"

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Write the keyfile that enables and sets the banner message.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
KEYFILE_CONTENT="[org/gnome/login-screen]
banner-message-enable=true
banner-message-text='${ESCAPED_BANNER}'"

if [[ ! -f "${KEYFILE}" ]] || [[ "$(cat "${KEYFILE}")" != "${KEYFILE_CONTENT}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${KEYFILE_CONTENT}" > "${KEYFILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${KEYFILE}"
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Lock the keys so non-privileged users cannot override them.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
LOCK_CONTENT="/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-enable
/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text"

if [[ ! -f "${LOCKFILE}" ]] || [[ "$(cat "${LOCKFILE}")" != "${LOCK_CONTENT}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${LOCK_CONTENT}" > "${LOCKFILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${LOCKFILE}"
fi

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compile the dconf databases so the changes take effect and persist.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
dconf update

echo "GDM graphical login warning banner configured and locked successfully."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#

set -euo pipefail

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
PWQUALITY_DIR="/etc/security"
SYSTEM_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/system-auth"
PASSWORD_AUTH="/etc/pam.d/password-auth"

# Tunable parameters
DENY=5
UNLOCK_TIME=900
FAIL_INTERVAL=900

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: ensure a "key = value" line exists (uncommented) in a config file
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------
set_conf_kv() {
    local file="$1" key="$2" value="$3"

    [ -f "$file" ] || touch "$file"

    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}([[:space:]]*=.*|[[:space:]]*)$" "$file" \
       || grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "$file"; then
        # Replace existing (commented or not) line
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*$|${key} = ${value}|" "$file"
    else
        printf '%s = %s\n' "$key" "$value" >> "$file"
    fi
}

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Configure /etc/security/faillock.conf
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------
set_conf_kv "$FAILLOCK_CONF" "deny"          "$DENY"
set_conf_kv "$FAILLOCK_CONF" "unlock_time"   "$UNLOCK_TIME"
set_conf_kv "$FAILLOCK_CONF" "fail_interval" "$FAIL_INTERVAL"

# Ensure root may also be locked and entries are flushed across reboots
if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*silent' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
    sed -ri 's|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*silent.*$|silent|' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
else
    echo "silent" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
fi

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Ensure pam_faillock is engaged in the auth stacks
#    On RHEL 8, authselect is the recommended mechanism. Prefer it when
#    a profile is in use, otherwise edit PAM files directly.
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------

configure_with_authselect() {
    local current
    current="$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)"
    [ -n "$current" ] || return 1

    # Enable the faillock feature (with-faillock) idempotently
    if ! authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
        authselect enable-feature with-faillock
    fi
    authselect apply-changes -b
    return 0
}

configure_pam_file_directly() {
    local file="$1"

    [ -f "$file" ] || return 0

    # Backup once
    [ -f "${file}.faillock.bak" ] || cp -a "$file" "${file}.faillock.bak"

    # --- auth: preauth (counts failures before authentication proceeds) ---
    if ! grep -Eq '^\s*auth\s+required\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+preauth' "$file"; then
        # Insert preauth line before the first auth pam_unix line
        if grep -Eq '^\s*auth\s+.*pam_unix\.so' "$file"; then
            sed -ri '0,/^\s*auth\s+.*pam_unix\.so/{
                /^\s*auth\s+.*pam_unix\.so/i auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny='"$DENY"' unlock_time='"$UNLOCK_TIME"' fail_interval='"$FAIL_INTERVAL"'
            }' "$file"
        else
            sed -ri '1i auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny='"$DENY"' unlock_time='"$UNLOCK_TIME"' fail_interval='"$FAIL_INTERVAL"'' "$file"
        fi
    fi

    # --- auth: authfail (records a failure on auth deny) ---
    if ! grep -Eq '^\s*auth\s+\[default=die\]\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+authfail' "$file"; then
        # Insert authfail right after the last auth pam_unix line
        sed -ri '/^\s*auth\s+.*pam_unix\.so/a auth        [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail deny='"$DENY"' unlock_time='"$UNLOCK_TIME"' fail_interval='"$FAIL_INTERVAL"'' "$file"
    fi

    # --- account: required pam_faillock.so ---
    if ! grep -Eq '^\s*account\s+required\s+pam_faillock\.so' "$file"; then
        if grep -Eq '^\s*account\s+.*pam_unix\.so' "$file"; then
            sed -ri '0,/^\s*account\s+.*pam_unix\.so/{
                /^\s*account\s+.*pam_unix\.so/i account     required      pam_faillock.so
            }' "$file"
        else
            echo "account     required      pam_faillock.so" >> "$file"
        fi
    fi
}

if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1 && authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! configure_with_authselect; then
        configure_pam_file_directly "$SYSTEM_AUTH"
        configure_pam_file_directly "$PASSWORD_AUTH"
    fi
else
    configure_pam_file_directly "$SYSTEM_AUTH"
    configure_pam_file_directly "$PASSWORD_AUTH"
fi

echo "pam_faillock configuration applied successfully."
echo "Settings: deny=${DENY}, unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}, fail_interval=${FAIL_INTERVAL}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Idempotent remediation: ensure account-lockout events are logged via faillock.
# On RHEL 8, faillock is configured in /etc/security/faillock.conf and must have
# the "audit" option enabled so that lockout events are recorded to the audit log.
#
set -euo pipefail

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Ensure the faillock.conf file exists.
if [[ ! -f "${FAILLOCK_CONF}" ]]; then
    install -m 0644 -o root -g root /dev/null "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
fi

# Function to ensure a given keyword option is present and uncommented in faillock.conf.
ensure_faillock_option() {
    local key="$1"

    # Match lines that are exactly the bare keyword (optionally commented/whitespaced).
    if grep -Eq "^\s*#?\s*${key}\s*$" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        # Normalize any existing (possibly commented) occurrence to the active keyword.
        sed -ri "s|^\s*#?\s*${key}\s*$|${key}|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    else
        # Append the keyword if it is entirely absent.
        printf '%s\n' "${key}" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi
}

# Enable audit logging of faillock events (records lockouts to the audit log).
ensure_faillock_option "audit"

# Ensure the directory used to store failure records exists and is referenced.
# (silent removal of accidental duplicate "audit" lines, keeping the first)
awk '
    /^audit$/ {
        if (seen_audit) next
        seen_audit = 1
    }
    { print }
' "${FAILLOCK_CONF}" > "${FAILLOCK_CONF}.tmp" && mv "${FAILLOCK_CONF}.tmp" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
chmod 0644 "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
chown root:root "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"

# Ensure pam_faillock is enabled in the PAM stack so that faillock.conf is honored.
# Use authselect if it is managing the profile; otherwise fall back to verifying
# pam_faillock presence in the relevant PAM config files.
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1 && authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Enable the with-faillock feature idempotently.
    if ! authselect current | grep -qw "with-faillock"; then
        authselect enable-feature with-faillock
        authselect apply-changes
    fi
else
    for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
        [[ -f "${pam_file}" ]] || continue
        if ! grep -q "pam_faillock.so" "${pam_file}"; then
            # Insert preauth and authfail lines around pam_unix for the auth stack.
            sed -ri \
                -e '0,/^auth\s+.*pam_unix\.so/{/^auth\s+.*pam_unix\.so/i auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth
}' \
                -e '/^auth\s+.*pam_unix\.so/a auth        [default=die] pam_faillock.so authfail' \
                "${pam_file}"
            # Add account stanza for faillock if missing.
            if ! grep -q "account.*pam_faillock.so" "${pam_file}"; then
                sed -ri '/^account\s+.*pam_unix\.so/i account     required      pam_faillock.so' "${pam_file}"
            fi
        fi
    done
fi

echo "faillock audit logging of account-lockout events is configured."

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

if ! command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then

    local value="$2"
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then

#
# Enable failed-attempt lockout enforcement for the root account on RHEL 8.
# This script is idempotent and ensures the setting persists across reboots.
#
set -euo pipefail

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Ensure faillock.conf exists
if [[ ! -f "${FAILLOCK_CONF}" ]]; then
    touch "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    chmod 0644 "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    chown root:root "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
fi

# RHEL 8 uses pam_faillock via authselect. The proper, persistent way to
# enable faillock is to enable the 'with-faillock' authselect feature, then
# configure 'even_deny_root' (or 'deny_root') so the root account is also
# subject to the lockout policy.

# 1. Make sure faillock is enabled in the authselect profile.
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Determine current profile; if none selected, select sssd with faillock.
    if authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! authselect current | grep -qw "with-faillock"; then
            authselect enable-feature with-faillock
        fi
    else
        authselect select sssd with-faillock --force
    fi
    # Apply changes to the live PAM configuration.
    authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# 2. Ensure the faillock.conf enforces lockout for root via 'even_deny_root'.
#    'even_deny_root' makes the root account subject to the same lockout policy.
set_faillock_option() {
    local key="$1"
    # Uncomment / add the option (it's a flag with no value).
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*$" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*$|${key}|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    else

    fi
}

        sed -i -E "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*|${key} = ${val}|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    else
        printf '%s = %s\n' "${key}" "${val}" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi
}

set_faillock_option "even_deny_root"

set_faillock_kv "unlock_time" "600"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Enable failed-attempt lockout enforcement for the root account on RHEL 8.
# This script is idempotent and ensures the setting persists across reboots.
#
set -euo pipefail

FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

# Ensure faillock.conf exists
if [[ ! -f "${FAILLOCK_CONF}" ]]; then
    touch "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    chmod 0644 "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    chown root:root "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
fi

# RHEL 8 uses pam_faillock via authselect. The proper, persistent way to
# enable faillock is to enable the 'with-faillock' authselect feature, then
# configure 'even_deny_root' (or 'deny_root') so the root account is also
# subject to the lockout policy.

# 1. Make sure faillock is enabled in the authselect profile.
if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Determine current profile; if none selected, select sssd with faillock.
    if authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! authselect current | grep -qw "with-faillock"; then
            authselect enable-feature with-faillock
        fi
    else
        authselect select sssd with-faillock --force
    fi
    # Apply changes to the live PAM configuration.
    authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

# 2. Ensure the faillock.conf enforces lockout for root via 'even_deny_root'.
#    'even_deny_root' makes the root account subject to the same lockout policy.
set_faillock_option() {
    local key="$1"
    # Uncomment / add the option (it's a flag with no value).
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*$" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*$|${key}|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    else
        printf '%s\n' "${key}" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi
}

set_faillock_kv() {
    local key="$1" val="$2"
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*|${key} = ${val}|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    else
        printf '%s = %s\n' "${key}" "${val}" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi
}

# Enable root lockout enforcement.
set_faillock_option "even_deny_root"

# Ensure sensible lockout parameters exist (idempotently set defaults if absent).
set_faillock_kv "deny" "3"
set_faillock_kv "unlock_time" "600"
set_faillock_kv "fail_interval" "900"

# 3. As a fallback for systems where the PAM modules reference arguments
#    directly (not faillock.conf), ensure even_deny_root is present on the
#    pam_faillock lines in the local PAM auth files (authselect custom or
#    non-authselect systems).
for pamfile in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    [[ -f "${pamfile}" ]] || continue
    if grep -q "pam_faillock.so" "${pamfile}"; then
        # Add even_deny_root to faillock lines that don't already have it.
        sed -i -E '/pam_faillock\.so/ { /even_deny_root/! s/(pam_faillock\.so[^\n]*)/\1 even_deny_root/ }' "${pamfile}"
    fi
done

echo "Root account failed-attempt lockout enforcement configured."

#
# This is achieved by:
#   1. Setting "silent" in /etc/security/faillock.conf (authselect/modern path).
#   2. Ensuring the pam_faillock entries in the PAM stacks carry the

#
set -euo pipefail

PAM_FILES=("/etc/pam.d/system-auth" "/etc/pam.d/password-auth")

###############################################################################
# 1. Configure /etc/security/faillock.conf
###############################################################################
configure_faillock_conf() {
    # Create the file if it does not exist.
    if [[ ! -f "${FAILLOCK_CONF}" ]]; then
        touch "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
        chmod 0644 "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi

    # If a 'silent' directive already exists (commented or not), normalize it.
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*silent([[:space:]]|$)' "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        sed -i -E 's@^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*silent([[:space:]]|$).*@silent@' "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    else
        # Append a clean entry.
        printf '%s\n' 'silent' >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi
}

###############################################################################
# 2. Ensure pam_faillock lines carry the 'silent' option (idempotent)
###############################################################################
ensure_silent_in_pam() {
    local file="$1"

    [[ -f "${file}" ]] || return 0

    # Process every line that references pam_faillock.so but does not yet
    # contain the 'silent' option.
    if grep -Eq 'pam_faillock\.so' "${file}"; then
        # Append 'silent' to pam_faillock.so lines lacking it.
        sed -i -E '/pam_faillock\.so/{
            /\bsilent\b/!s/(pam_faillock\.so[[:space:]].*)$/\1 silent/
            /\bsilent\b/!s/(pam_faillock\.so)[[:space:]]*$/\1 silent/
        }' "${file}"
    fi
}

###############################################################################
# Apply
###############################################################################
configure_faillock_conf

if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # On authselect-managed systems, faillock.conf is the authoritative source.
    # Re-apply the current profile so changes propagate consistently, if possible.
    if authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
fi

# Also enforce consistency directly in the PAM stacks (covers non-authselect
# setups and ensures the option is present regardless of profile state).
for pam_file in "${PAM_FILES[@]}"; do
    ensure_silent_in_pam "${pam_file}"
done

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Configure account lockout policy on RHEL 8 using faillock.
# - Locks accounts after consecutive failed authentication attempts
# - Automatically unlocks after a defined interval (>= 900 seconds)
#
set -euo pipefail

UNLOCK_TIME=900
DENY=3
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

if [[ "${UNLOCK_TIME}" -lt 900 ]]; then
    echo "unlock_time must be >= 900 seconds" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#######################################
# 1. Configure /etc/security/faillock.conf (authselect/RHEL 8 preferred)
#######################################
configure_faillock_conf() {
    [[ -f "${FAILLOCK_CONF}" ]] || touch "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"

    set_kv() {
        local key="$1" val="$2"
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
            sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*|${key} = ${val}|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
        elif grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*$" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
            sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*$|${key} = ${val}|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
        else
            echo "${key} = ${val}" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
        fi
    }

    set_kv "deny" "${DENY}"
    set_kv "unlock_time" "${UNLOCK_TIME}"

    # Ensure faillock key exists (without value) so PAM uses faillock.conf
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*faillock[[:space:]]*$" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*faillock[[:space:]]*$|faillock|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    elif ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*faillock[[:space:]]*$" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        echo "faillock" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi
}

#######################################
# 2. Enable faillock via authselect if in use
#######################################
configure_authselect() {
    local current
    if ! command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        return 1
    fi
    if ! current="$(authselect current 2>/dev/null)"; then
        return 1
    fi

    local profile
    profile="$(echo "${current}" | awk -F'Profile ID: ' 'NR==1{print $2}' | tr -d '[:space:]')"
    [[ -n "${profile}" ]] || return 1

    if ! authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -qw "with-faillock"; then
        authselect enable-feature with-faillock
    fi

    authselect apply-changes -b 2>/dev/null || authselect apply-changes
    return 0
}

#######################################
# 3. Fallback: edit PAM files directly if authselect not active
#######################################
configure_pam_direct() {
    local pam_files=(/etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth)
    local f

    for f in "${pam_files[@]}"; do
        [[ -f "${f}" ]] || continue

        # preauth line in auth section
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*auth\b.*pam_faillock\.so.*preauth" "${f}"; then
            sed -ri "0,/^auth\b/s//auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}\nauth/" "${f}"
        else
            sed -ri "s|^([[:space:]]*auth[[:space:]]+[^#]*pam_faillock\.so[[:space:]]+preauth.*)|auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}|" "${f}"
        fi

        # authfail line after pam_unix
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*auth\b.*pam_faillock\.so.*authfail" "${f}"; then
            sed -ri "/^auth\b.*pam_unix\.so/a auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}" "${f}"
        else
            sed -ri "s|^([[:space:]]*auth[[:space:]]+[^#]*pam_faillock\.so[[:space:]]+authfail.*)|auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}|" "${f}"
        fi

        # account section
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*account\b.*pam_faillock\.so" "${f}"; then
            sed -ri "0,/^account\b/s//account     required                                     pam_faillock.so\naccount/" "${f}"
        fi
    done
}

#######################################
# Main
#######################################
configure_faillock_conf

if ! configure_authselect; then
    echo "authselect not in use or unavailable; configuring PAM directly." >&2
    configure_pam_direct
fi

echo "Account lockout policy applied: deny=${DENY}, unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}s"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Configure account lockout policy on RHEL 8 using faillock.
# - Locks accounts after consecutive failed authentication attempts
# - Automatically unlocks after a defined interval (>= 900 seconds)
#
set -euo pipefail

UNLOCK_TIME=900
DENY=3
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"

if [[ "${UNLOCK_TIME}" -lt 900 ]]; then
    echo "unlock_time must be >= 900 seconds" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#######################################
# 1. Configure /etc/security/faillock.conf (authselect/RHEL 8 preferred)
#######################################
configure_faillock_conf() {
    [[ -f "${FAILLOCK_CONF}" ]] || touch "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"

    set_kv() {
        local key="$1" val="$2"
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
            sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=.*|${key} = ${val}|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
        elif grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*$" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
            sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*$|${key} = ${val}|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
        else
            echo "${key} = ${val}" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
        fi
    }

    set_kv "deny" "${DENY}"
    set_kv "unlock_time" "${UNLOCK_TIME}"

    # Ensure faillock key exists (without value) so PAM uses faillock.conf
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*faillock[[:space:]]*$" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*faillock[[:space:]]*$|faillock|" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    elif ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*faillock[[:space:]]*$" "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"; then
        echo "faillock" >> "${FAILLOCK_CONF}"
    fi
}

#######################################
# 2. Enable faillock via authselect if in use
#######################################
configure_authselect() {
    local current
    if ! command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        return 1
    fi
    if ! current="$(authselect current 2>/dev/null)"; then
        return 1
    fi

    local profile
    profile="$(echo "${current}" | awk -F'Profile ID: ' 'NR==1{print $2}' | tr -d '[:space:]')"
    [[ -n "${profile}" ]] || return 1

    if ! authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -qw "with-faillock"; then
        authselect enable-feature with-faillock
    fi

    authselect apply-changes -b 2>/dev/null || authselect apply-changes
    return 0
}

#######################################
# 3. Fallback: edit PAM files directly if authselect not active
#######################################
configure_pam_direct() {
    local pam_files=(/etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth)
    local f

    for f in "${pam_files[@]}"; do
        [[ -f "${f}" ]] || continue

        # preauth line in auth section
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*auth\b.*pam_faillock\.so.*preauth" "${f}"; then
            sed -ri "0,/^auth\b/s//auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}\nauth/" "${f}"
        else
            sed -ri "s|^([[:space:]]*auth[[:space:]]+[^#]*pam_faillock\.so[[:space:]]+preauth.*)|auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth silent deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}|" "${f}"
        fi

        # authfail line after pam_unix
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*auth\b.*pam_faillock\.so.*authfail" "${f}"; then
            sed -ri "/^auth\b.*pam_unix\.so/a auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}" "${f}"
        else
            sed -ri "s|^([[:space:]]*auth[[:space:]]+[^#]*pam_faillock\.so[[:space:]]+authfail.*)|auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail deny=${DENY} unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}|" "${f}"
        fi

        # account section
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*account\b.*pam_faillock\.so" "${f}"; then
            sed -ri "0,/^account\b/s//account     required                                     pam_faillock.so\naccount/" "${f}"
        fi
    done
}

#######################################
# Main
#######################################
configure_faillock_conf

if ! configure_authselect; then
    echo "authselect not in use or unavailable; configuring PAM directly." >&2
    configure_pam_direct
fi

echo "Account lockout policy applied: deny=${DENY}, unlock_time=${UNLOCK_TIME}s"
```
