#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Enforce a system-wide password-quality policy on RHEL 8 requiring that
# every newly set or changed password contains at least one numeric digit.
#
# This is achieved by setting dcredit = -1 in /etc/security/pwquality.conf
# A negative value (-1) mandates at least one character of that class.
#
# The script is idempotent: repeated runs converge to the same desired state.

set -euo pipefail

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PARAM="dcredit"
VALUE="-1"

# Ensure the configuration file exists.
if [[ ! -f "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" ]]; then
    touch "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
    chmod 0644 "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
    chown root:root "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Build a regex that matches any (commented or uncommented) dcredit setting.
# Leading whitespace and optional '#' are tolerated.
MATCH_REGEX='^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*dcredit[[:space:]]*='

DESIRED_LINE="${PARAM} = ${VALUE}"

if grep -Eq "${MATCH_REGEX}" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"; then
    # A dcredit entry (active or commented) exists; normalize it to the desired value.
    # Replace the first matching line and remove any additional matching lines.
    awk -v desired="${DESIRED_LINE}" '
        BEGIN { done = 0 }
        /^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*dcredit[[:space:]]*=/ {
            if (done == 0) {
                print desired
                done = 1
            }
            next
        }
        { print }
    ' "${PWQUALITY_CONF}" > "${PWQUALITY_CONF}.tmp"

    mv "${PWQUALITY_CONF}.tmp" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
else
    # No dcredit entry present; append the desired setting.
    printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_LINE}" >> "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Re-assert correct ownership and permissions.
chown root:root "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
chmod 0644 "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"

# Verify the result.
if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${PWQUALITY_CONF}"; then
    echo "Password-quality policy enforced: ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} in ${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to enforce ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} in ${PWQUALITY_CONF}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: enforce password complexity requiring characters from at least
# four distinct character classes (upper, lower, digit, special) on RHEL 8.
#
# This is achieved by setting minclass = 4 in /etc/security/pwquality.conf
# (and any drop-in files under /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/).
#
# The script is idempotent: repeated runs converge to the same desired state.

set -euo pipefail

CONF_FILE="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
CONF_DIR="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"
PARAM="minclass"
VALUE="4"

# Must run as root to modify system configuration.
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the libpwquality package is installed so PAM enforces the policy.
if command -v rpm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! rpm -q libpwquality >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Installing libpwquality..."
        if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            dnf -y install libpwquality
        elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            yum -y install libpwquality
        fi
    fi
fi

# Ensure the main configuration file exists.
if [[ ! -f "$CONF_FILE" ]]; then
    touch "$CONF_FILE"
    chmod 0644 "$CONF_FILE"
fi

# Remove any conflicting minclass settings from drop-in files so the main
# file is authoritative and the policy is enforced consistently.
if [[ -d "$CONF_DIR" ]]; then
    shopt -s nullglob
    for dropin in "$CONF_DIR"/*.conf; do
        if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*minclass[[:space:]]*=' "$dropin"; then
            sed -ri "/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*minclass[[:space:]]*=.*/d" "$dropin"
        fi
    done
    shopt -u nullglob
fi

# Apply the setting idempotently in the main configuration file.
if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "$CONF_FILE"; then
    # Update existing active setting to the desired value.
    sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=.*|${PARAM} = ${VALUE}|" "$CONF_FILE"
elif grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "$CONF_FILE"; then
    # Uncomment and set a commented-out setting.
    sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=.*|${PARAM} = ${VALUE}|" "$CONF_FILE"
else
    # Append the setting if not present at all.
    printf '%s = %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$CONF_FILE"
fi

# Verify the resulting active configuration.
if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "$CONF_FILE"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} is enforced in ${CONF_FILE}."
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to enforce ${PARAM} = ${VALUE} in ${CONF_FILE}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#
# Enforce a minimum of 5000 SHA password-hashing rounds system-wide on RHEL 8.
# Idempotent remediation script.

set -euo pipefail

MIN_ROUNDS=5000
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"

###############################################################################
# 1. Configure /etc/login.defs (SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS / SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS)
###############################################################################

if [ ! -f "$LOGIN_DEFS" ]; then
    touch "$LOGIN_DEFS"
fi

set_login_defs() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"

    # Find an active (non-commented) setting for the key
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+" "$file"; then
        current="$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+" "$file" | tail -n1 | awk '{print $2}')"
        if [[ "$current" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [ "$current" -ge "$MIN_ROUNDS" ]; then
            # Already compliant; normalize to a single definitive line
            sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+.*|${key} ${value}|" "$file"
        else
            sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+.*|${key} ${value}|" "$file"
        fi
        # Remove any duplicate active definitions, keeping the first
        awk -v k="$key" '
            $1==k && seen { next }
            $1==k { seen=1 }
            { print }
        ' "$file" > "${file}.tmp" && mv "${file}.tmp" "$file"
    else
        printf '%s %s\n' "$key" "$value" >> "$file"
    fi
}

set_login_defs "SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS" "$MIN_ROUNDS" "$LOGIN_DEFS"
set_login_defs "SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS" "$MIN_ROUNDS" "$LOGIN_DEFS"

###############################################################################
# 2. Configure PAM (pam_unix.so rounds) via authselect if available,
#    otherwise edit the PAM files directly.
###############################################################################

update_pam_file() {
    local file="$1"
    [ -f "$file" ] || return 0

    # Process password lines using pam_unix.so
    local tmp
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    awk -v rounds="$MIN_ROUNDS" '
        # Only modify password lines invoking pam_unix.so
        ($1=="password") && ($0 ~ /pam_unix\.so/) {
            line=$0
            if (line ~ /rounds=[0-9]+/) {
                # Replace existing rounds value
                gsub(/rounds=[0-9]+/, "rounds=" rounds, line)
            } else {
                # Append rounds= to the end of the line
                line=line " rounds=" rounds
            }
            print line
            next
        }
        { print }
    ' "$file" > "$tmp"

    if ! cmp -s "$file" "$tmp"; then
        cat "$tmp" > "$file"
    fi
    rm -f "$tmp"
}

if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1 && authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Edit custom/templated PAM stacks then re-apply
    for f in /etc/authselect/password-auth /etc/authselect/system-auth \
             /etc/authselect/custom/*/password-auth /etc/authselect/custom/*/system-auth; do
        [ -e "$f" ] && update_pam_file "$f"

    authselect apply-changes --backup=rounds.bak >/dev/null 2>&1 || \

    # Ensure generated stacks reflect the setting as a fallback
    update_pam_file /etc/pam.d/password-auth
    update_pam_file /etc/pam.d/system-auth
else
    update_pam_file /etc/pam.d/password-auth
    update_pam_file /etc/pam.d/system-auth
fi

echo "SHA password hashing rounds enforced to a minimum of ${MIN_ROUNDS}."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure systemd debug-shell service is fully disabled and masked.
# The debug-shell.service provides an unauthenticated root shell on tty9,
# which is a serious risk for systems where physical access is possible.
#
set -euo pipefail

SERVICE="debug-shell.service"
LOG_TAG="debug-shell-remediation"

log() {
    printf '%s [%s] %s\n' "$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z')" "$LOG_TAG" "$*"
}

# Must run as root to alter systemd unit state.
if [[ "${EUID:-$(id -u)}" -ne 0 ]]; then
    log "ERROR: This script must be run as root."
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure systemctl exists.
if ! command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "ERROR: systemctl not found; this script targets systemd-based systems."
    exit 1
fi

# Stop the service if it is currently running (idempotent: no error if inactive).
if systemctl is-active --quiet "$SERVICE"; then
    log "Stopping active ${SERVICE}."
    systemctl stop "$SERVICE" || log "WARNING: failed to stop ${SERVICE}."
else
    log "${SERVICE} is not active."
fi

# Disable the service so it won't be enabled via dependencies (best-effort).
if systemctl is-enabled --quiet "$SERVICE" 2>/dev/null; then
    log "Disabling ${SERVICE}."
    systemctl disable "$SERVICE" || log "WARNING: failed to disable ${SERVICE}."
else
    log "${SERVICE} is not enabled (or has no install info)."
fi

# Mask the service. Masking links the unit to /dev/null, preventing it from
# being started either manually or as a dependency. This is the authoritative
# control and persists across reboots.
MASK_STATE="$(systemctl is-enabled "$SERVICE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [[ "$MASK_STATE" == "masked" ]]; then
    log "${SERVICE} is already masked; no change needed."
else
    log "Masking ${SERVICE}."
    systemctl mask "$SERVICE"
fi

# Reload systemd to ensure state is applied.
systemctl daemon-reload || log "WARNING: daemon-reload returned non-zero."

# Verification.
FINAL_STATE="$(systemctl is-enabled "$SERVICE" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [[ "$FINAL_STATE" == "masked" ]]; then
    log "SUCCESS: ${SERVICE} is masked and cannot be started."
else
    log "ERROR: ${SERVICE} is not masked (state: '${FINAL_STATE:-unknown}')."
    exit 1
fi

if systemctl is-active --quiet "$SERVICE"; then
    log "ERROR: ${SERVICE} is still active after remediation."
    exit 1
fi

log "Remediation complete."
exit 0

# ensure the burst action is set to none for the rapid burst sequence.

if [ -f "$CONF_FILE" ]; then
    if grep -Eq '^\s*#?\s*CtrlAltDelBurstAction\s*=' "$CONF_FILE"; then

        else
            printf '\n[Manager]\n%s\n' "$SETTING" >> "$CONF_FILE"
        fi
    fi

    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$CONF_FILE")"
    printf '[Manager]\n%s\n' "$SETTING" > "$CONF_FILE"
fi

systemctl daemon-reexec

    echo "CtrlAltDelBurstAction is set to none in ${CONF_FILE}."
else
    echo "Failed to enforce CtrlAltDelBurstAction=none." >&2

fi

# Mask the ctrl-alt-del.target so the Ctrl-Alt-Del key sequence does not
# trigger a reboot. Masking creates a symlink to /dev/null which survives
# system updates and persists across reboots.

TARGET="ctrl-alt-del.target"

if [ -L "${LINK}" ] && [ "$(readlink -f "${LINK}")" = "/dev/null" ]; then

CONFIG_FILE="/etc/systemd/logind.conf"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/systemd/logind.conf.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/50-idle-termination.conf"

# Idle limit: 900 seconds (15 minutes) of inactivity before session termination.
IDLE_SECS="900"
IDLE_ACTION="lock"

if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "${DROPIN_DIR}"

# Write/refresh the drop-in configuration idempotently.
cat > "${DROPIN_FILE}" <<EOF
[Login]
StopIdleSessionSec=${IDLE_SECS}
IdleAction=${IDLE_ACTION}
IdleActionSec=${IDLE_SECS}
EOF

chmod 0644 "${DROPIN_FILE}"
chown root:root "${DROPIN_FILE}"

# Ensure no conflicting active (uncommented) settings exist in the main config.
if [[ -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
    for key in StopIdleSessionSec IdleAction IdleActionSec; do
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "${CONFIG_FILE}"; then
            sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*(${key}[[:space:]]*=.*)$/# \1/" "${CONFIG_FILE}"
        fi
    done

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

CONFIG_FILE="/etc/systemd/logind.conf"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/systemd/logind.conf.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/50-idle-termination.conf"

# Idle limit: 900 seconds (15 minutes) of inactivity before session termination.
IDLE_SECS="900"
IDLE_ACTION="lock"

if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "${DROPIN_DIR}"

# Write/refresh the drop-in configuration idempotently.
cat > "${DROPIN_FILE}" <<EOF
[Login]
StopIdleSessionSec=${IDLE_SECS}
IdleAction=${IDLE_ACTION}
IdleActionSec=${IDLE_SECS}
EOF

chmod 0644 "${DROPIN_FILE}"
chown root:root "${DROPIN_FILE}"

# Ensure no conflicting active (uncommented) settings exist in the main config.
if [[ -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
    for key in StopIdleSessionSec IdleAction IdleActionSec; do
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]*=" "${CONFIG_FILE}"; then
            sed -ri "s/^[[:space:]]*(${key}[[:space:]]*=.*)$/# \1/" "${CONFIG_FILE}"
        fi
    done
fi

# Reload systemd-logind so changes take effect and persist across reboots.
if systemctl is-active --quiet systemd-logind; then
    systemctl restart systemd-logind
else
    systemctl start systemd-logind || true
fi

echo "systemd-logind idle session termination configured: ${IDLE_SECS}s -> ${IDLE_ACTION}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MAX_DAYS=365
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"

# Ensure PASS_MAX_DAYS default is set for future accounts
if grep -Eq '^\s*PASS_MAX_DAYS\s+' "$LOGIN_DEFS"; then
    current_default=$(grep -E '^\s*PASS_MAX_DAYS\s+' "$LOGIN_DEFS" | awk '{print $2}' | tail -n1)
    if [ "$current_default" != "$MAX_DAYS" ]; then
        sed -i -E "s/^\s*PASS_MAX_DAYS\s+.*/PASS_MAX_DAYS\t$MAX_DAYS/" "$LOGIN_DEFS"
    fi
else
    printf 'PASS_MAX_DAYS\t%s\n' "$MAX_DAYS" >> "$LOGIN_DEFS"
fi

# Iterate over all local user accounts in /etc/passwd
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ _ shell; do
    # Skip accounts without a valid name
    [ -z "$username" ] && continue

    # Determine current maximum password age from /etc/shadow
    shadow_entry=$(getent shadow "$username" 2>/dev/null || true)
    [ -z "$shadow_entry" ] && continue

    # Field 5 (1-indexed) of shadow is max days
    current_max=$(printf '%s' "$shadow_entry" | awk -F: '{print $5}')

    # Only adjust accounts that are non-compliant:
    # empty, non-numeric, or greater than allowed maximum
    if ! [[ "$current_max" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [ "$current_max" -gt "$MAX_DAYS" ]; then
        chage --maxdays "$MAX_DAYS" "$username"
        echo "Updated $username: PASS_MAX_DAYS set to $MAX_DAYS"
    fi
done < /etc/passwd

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Enforce a minimum password age of 1 day for all existing local user accounts.
# Idempotent: only modifies accounts whose current minimum age is < 1.
#
set -euo pipefail

MIN_DAYS=1

# Ensure required tools are present
for cmd in chage passwd getent; do
    if ! command -v "$cmd" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "ERROR: required command '$cmd' not found." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

# Must be run as root to modify password aging
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: this script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Also set the default for newly created users in /etc/login.defs
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"
if [[ -f "$LOGIN_DEFS" ]]; then
    if grep -Eq '^\s*PASS_MIN_DAYS\s+' "$LOGIN_DEFS"; then
        current_default="$(awk '/^[[:space:]]*PASS_MIN_DAYS[[:space:]]+/ {print $2; exit}' "$LOGIN_DEFS")"
        if [[ "${current_default:-0}" -lt "$MIN_DAYS" ]]; then
            sed -ri "s/^([[:space:]]*PASS_MIN_DAYS[[:space:]]+).*/\1${MIN_DAYS}/" "$LOGIN_DEFS"
            echo "Updated PASS_MIN_DAYS in ${LOGIN_DEFS} to ${MIN_DAYS}."
        fi
    else
        echo "PASS_MIN_DAYS ${MIN_DAYS}" >> "$LOGIN_DEFS"
        echo "Added PASS_MIN_DAYS ${MIN_DAYS} to ${LOGIN_DEFS}."
    fi
fi

changed=0

# Iterate over all local accounts defined in /etc/passwd
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ _ _; do
    [[ -z "$username" ]] && continue

    # Retrieve the minimum days between password changes from shadow (field 4)
    shadow_entry="$(getent shadow "$username" || true)"
    [[ -z "$shadow_entry" ]] && continue

    min_field="$(echo "$shadow_entry" | cut -d: -f4)"

    # Treat empty/unset as non-compliant (effectively 0)
    if [[ -z "$min_field" || ! "$min_field" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        min_field=0
    fi

    if [[ "$min_field" -lt "$MIN_DAYS" ]]; then
        if chage --mindays "$MIN_DAYS" "$username"; then
            echo "Set minimum password age to ${MIN_DAYS} day(s) for user '${username}' (was ${min_field})."
            changed=$((changed + 1))
        else
            echo "WARNING: failed to set minimum password age for user '${username}'." >&2
        fi
    fi
done < <(getent passwd)

if [[ "$changed" -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "All local accounts already compliant with minimum password age of ${MIN_DAYS} day(s)."
else
    echo "Remediation complete: ${changed} account(s) updated."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Remediation: refuse logins to accounts with blank/empty passwords on RHEL 8.
# The "nullok" option on pam_unix.so (in system-auth and password-auth) allows
# empty-password authentication; remove it to enforce the requirement.

# On RHEL 8, /etc/pam.d/system-auth and /etc/pam.d/password-auth are typically
# symlinks managed by authselect. Determine how PAM is configured.

remediate_file() {
    local file="$1"
    [ -f "$file" ] || return 0
    if grep -qE '\bnullok\b' "$file"; then
        # Remove nullok (and any leading whitespace it leaves) on pam_unix lines
        sed -ri 's/(\s+)nullok\b//g; s/\bnullok\b\s*//g' "$file"
    fi
}

if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1 && authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # System is managed by authselect.
    # Check whether the "with-nullok" feature is enabled and disable it.
    if authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -qw 'with-nullok'; then
        authselect disable-feature with-nullok
        authselect apply-changes -b
    fi

    # In case nullok still remains in the underlying generated files
    # (e.g., custom profile), strip it and re-apply.
    for f in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth \
             /etc/authselect/custom/*/system-auth \
             /etc/authselect/custom/*/password-auth; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        # Resolve symlink targets that point into authselect config
        target="$f"
        if [ -L "$f" ]; then
            target="$(readlink -f "$f")"
        fi
        if grep -qE '\bnullok\b' "$target" 2>/dev/null; then
            remediate_file "$target"
        fi
    done

    # Re-apply to regenerate the live PAM files from the (now clean) profile.
    if authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        authselect apply-changes -b || true
    fi
else
    # Not managed by authselect: edit the PAM files directly.
    remediate_file /etc/pam.d/system-auth
    remediate_file /etc/pam.d/password-auth
fi

# Final verification: ensure no nullok remains in the active PAM stacks.
for f in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    if [ -f "$f" ] && grep -qE '\bnullok\b' "$f"; then
        echo "ERROR: 'nullok' still present in $f" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

echo "Empty-password authentication has been disabled."

        sed -ri 's/(\s+)nullok\b//g; s/\bnullok\b\s*//g' "$file"
    fi
}

if command -v authselect >/dev/null 2>&1 && authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # System is managed by authselect.
    # Check whether the "with-nullok" feature is enabled and disable it.
    if authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -qw 'with-nullok'; then
        authselect disable-feature with-nullok
        authselect apply-changes -b
    fi

    # In case nullok still remains in the underlying generated files
    # (e.g., custom profile), strip it and re-apply.
    for f in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth \
             /etc/authselect/custom/*/system-auth \
             /etc/authselect/custom/*/password-auth; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        # Resolve symlink targets that point into authselect config
        target="$f"
        if [ -L "$f" ]; then
            target="$(readlink -f "$f")"
        fi
        if grep -qE '\bnullok\b' "$target" 2>/dev/null; then
            remediate_file "$target"
        fi
    done

    # Re-apply to regenerate the live PAM files from the (now clean) profile.
    if authselect current >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        authselect apply-changes -b || true
    fi
else
    # Not managed by authselect: edit the PAM files directly.
    remediate_file /etc/pam.d/system-auth
    remediate_file /etc/pam.d/password-auth
fi

# Final verification: ensure no nullok remains in the active PAM stacks.
for f in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    if [ -f "$f" ] && grep -qE '\bnullok\b' "$f"; then
        echo "ERROR: 'nullok' still present in $f" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

echo "Empty-password authentication has been disabled."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Restrict any single account to no more than 10 concurrent
# login sessions to mitigate DoS conditions from excessive simultaneous logins.
#
# This is enforced via the pam_limits "maxlogins" hard limit applied to all
# users ("*") in /etc/security/limits.conf, which persists across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.conf"
MAXLOGINS_VALUE=10
DESIRED_LINE="*               hard    maxlogins       ${MAXLOGINS_VALUE}"

# Ensure we are running as root.
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the limits file exists.
if [[ ! -f "$LIMITS_FILE" ]]; then
    touch "$LIMITS_FILE"
    chmod 0644 "$LIMITS_FILE"
fi

# Back up the file once if no backup exists.
if [[ ! -f "${LIMITS_FILE}.bak" ]]; then
    cp -p "$LIMITS_FILE" "${LIMITS_FILE}.bak"
fi

# Regex matching any existing "* hard maxlogins <value>" entry (ignoring whitespace).
EXISTING_REGEX='^[[:space:]]*\*[[:space:]]+hard[[:space:]]+maxlogins[[:space:]]+[0-9]+[[:space:]]*$'

if grep -Eq "$EXISTING_REGEX" "$LIMITS_FILE"; then
    # Replace any existing matching line(s) with the desired value, idempotently.
    # Remove all existing matching lines first, then append the canonical one.
    sed -i -E "/${EXISTING_REGEX//\//\\/}/d" "$LIMITS_FILE"
fi

# Append the desired line only if an exact match does not already exist.
if ! grep -Eq "$EXISTING_REGEX" "$LIMITS_FILE"; then
    printf '%s\n' "$DESIRED_LINE" >> "$LIMITS_FILE"
fi

# Verify the result.
if grep -Eq "$EXISTING_REGEX" "$LIMITS_FILE"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: Per-user concurrent session limit set to ${MAXLOGINS_VALUE} in ${LIMITS_FILE}."
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to apply the maxlogins limit to ${LIMITS_FILE}." >&2
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure all files and directories within each local interactive
# user's home directory are group-owned by a group of which that user is a member.
#

set -u

# Determine the UID_MIN value for interactive users from login.defs
UID_MIN=$(awk '/^\s*UID_MIN/{print $2}' /etc/login.defs)
[ -z "${UID_MIN:-}" ] && UID_MIN=1000

# Iterate over all local interactive users in /etc/passwd
awk -v uid_min="$UID_MIN" -F: '($3 >= uid_min && $3 != 65534) { print $1":"$4":"$6 }' /etc/passwd | \
while IFS=: read -r username primary_gid home_dir; do

    # Skip if no home directory defined
    [ -z "$home_dir" ] && continue

    # Skip if home directory does not exist or is not a directory
    [ -d "$home_dir" ] || continue

    # Skip system/non-interactive home directories
    case "$home_dir" in
        /|/dev/null|/bin|/sbin|/usr/sbin|/var/*|/run/*)
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Build the list of group IDs the user is a member of:
    #   - the user's primary group from /etc/passwd
    #   - any supplementary groups listing the user as a member
    declare -A valid_gids=()
    valid_gids["$primary_gid"]=1

    while IFS=: read -r g_name g_pass g_gid g_members; do
        # Check if the username appears in the comma-separated member list
        IFS=',' read -ra members <<< "$g_members"
        for m in "${members[@]}"; do
            if [ "$m" = "$username" ]; then
                valid_gids["$g_gid"]=1
            fi
        done
    done < /etc/group

    # Walk every file and directory within the home directory
    while IFS= read -r -d '' entry; do
        cur_gid=$(stat -c '%g' "$entry" 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$cur_gid" ] && continue

        # If the current group is not one of the user's groups, fix it
        if [ -z "${valid_gids[$cur_gid]:-}" ]; then
            chgrp -P "$primary_gid" "$entry"
        fi
    done < <(find "$home_dir" -print0 2>/dev/null)

    unset valid_gids
done

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: ensure non-hidden files/dirs in local interactive users'
# home directories are no more permissive than 0750.
#
set -u

# Determine the minimum UID for interactive accounts.
if [ -f /etc/login.defs ]; then
    UID_MIN=$(awk '/^[[:space:]]*UID_MIN[[:space:]]/{print $2}' /etc/login.defs | tail -1)
fi
UID_MIN=${UID_MIN:-1000}

# Collect local interactive users from /etc/passwd.
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ homedir shell; do
    # Skip accounts without a valid interactive shell.
    case "$shell" in
        */nologin|*/false|/bin/sync|/sbin/shutdown|/sbin/halt|"")
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Only local interactive users (UID >= UID_MIN and not nobody 65534).
    if [ "$uid" -lt "$UID_MIN" ] || [ "$uid" -eq 65534 ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Home directory must exist and be a directory.
    if [ -z "$homedir" ] || [ ! -d "$homedir" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Process non-hidden top-level entries and everything beneath them.
    # find with -path excludes dotfiles/dotdirs at the top level (and their contents).
    find "$homedir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name '.*' \
        \( -type f -o -type d \) -print0 2>/dev/null |
    while IFS= read -r -d '' entry; do
        # Apply to the entry and recurse into directories.
        find "$entry" \( -type f -o -type d \) -print0 2>/dev/null |
        while IFS= read -r -d '' target; do
            # Get current octal permissions.
            curperm=$(stat -c '%a' "$target" 2>/dev/null) || continue
            # Normalize to 4 digits for comparison.
            curperm_num=$((8#$curperm))
            # Mask: remove group-write and all "other" bits, keep within 0750.
            # 0750 = 0750. We must ensure no bits beyond 0750 are set.
            newperm_num=$(( curperm_num & 0750 ))
            if [ "$curperm_num" -ne "$newperm_num" ]; then
                # Remove group-write and all other permissions.
                chmod g-w,o-rwx "$target" 2>/dev/null
            fi
        done
    done
done < /etc/passwd

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediate permissions on per-user interactive shell initialization files.
# Ensure dot-files are no more permissive than 0740 (rwxr-----).
# Idempotent: only removes excess permission bits, never loosens.

set -u

# Mask of bits that are NOT allowed (anything beyond 0740).
# 0740 = rwxr-----   ; disallowed bits = 0037 (group w,x and all "other").
DISALLOWED=0037

# List of standard interactive shell init dot-files to check within a home dir.
INIT_FILES=(
    .bashrc
    .bash_profile
    .bash_login
    .profile
    .bash_logout
    .cshrc
    .tcshrc
    .login
    .logout
    .kshrc
    .zshrc
    .zlogin
    .zlogout
    .zprofile
    .zshenv
    .yashrc
    .yash_profile
    .mkshrc
    .shrc
)

tighten_file() {
    local file="$1"

    # Must exist and be a regular file (resolve symlinks: operate on target).
    [ -e "$file" ] || return 0

    # Get current octal mode.
    local mode
    mode=$(stat -L -c '%a' "$file" 2>/dev/null) || return 0
    [ -n "$mode" ] || return 0

    # Normalize to a number (strip any leading zeros for arithmetic).
    local cur=$((8#$mode))

    # Determine which disallowed bits are currently set.
    local excess=$(( cur & DISALLOWED ))

    if [ "$excess" -ne 0 ]; then
        # Remove only the disallowed bits; keep everything else as-is.
        local newmode=$(( cur & ~DISALLOWED ))
        printf -v newoct '%04o' "$newmode"
        echo "Tightening $file from $(printf '%04o' "$cur") to $newoct"
        chmod "$newoct" "$file"
    fi
}

process_home() {
    local home="$1"

    [ -n "$home" ] || return 0
    [ -d "$home" ] || return 0

    local f
    for f in "${INIT_FILES[@]}"; do
        tighten_file "$home/$f"
    done
}

# Process all human users (UID >= 1000) plus root, that have valid home dirs
# and login shells (skip nologin/false accounts other than root).
while IFS=':' read -r username _ uid _ _ homedir shell; do
    # Always include root.
    if [ "$uid" -eq 0 ]; then
        process_home "$homedir"
        continue
    fi

    # Human users: UID between 1000 and 60000 (typical RHEL range).
    if [ "$uid" -ge 1000 ] && [ "$uid" -le 60000 ]; then
        case "$shell" in
            */nologin|*/false|"")
                continue
                ;;
        esac
        process_home "$homedir"
    fi
done < <(getent passwd)

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Set default Bash shell file-creation umask to 027 system-wide.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.
#
set -euo pipefail

UMASK_VALUE="027"
TARGET_FILE="/etc/profile.d/set-umask.sh"

# Ensure we are running with sufficient privileges
if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

###############################################################################
# 1. Create/maintain a dedicated profile.d script to enforce the umask for
#    all new interactive login shells. This is the cleanest, idempotent place.
###############################################################################
TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
cat > "${TMP_FILE}" <<EOF
# Set default file-creation mask (umask) for all users.
# Managed by security remediation script. Do not edit manually.
umask ${UMASK_VALUE}
EOF

if [[ ! -f "${TARGET_FILE}" ]] || ! cmp -s "${TMP_FILE}" "${TARGET_FILE}"; then
    install -m 0644 -o root -g root "${TMP_FILE}" "${TARGET_FILE}"
    echo "Updated ${TARGET_FILE} to enforce umask ${UMASK_VALUE}."
else
    echo "${TARGET_FILE} already enforces umask ${UMASK_VALUE}."
fi
rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"

###############################################################################
# 2. Normalize any existing umask declarations in the core shell config files
#    so they do not override the desired value.
###############################################################################
normalize_umask_in_file() {
    local file="$1"

    [[ -f "${file}" ]] || return 0

    # If there is any active (non-commented) umask line that is not 027,
    # replace it with the correct value.
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+' "${file}"; then
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+0?${UMASK_VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${file}"; then
            sed -ri "s/^([[:space:]]*)umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+.*$/\1umask ${UMASK_VALUE}/" "${file}"
            echo "Corrected existing umask declaration in ${file}."
        else
            echo "${file} already declares umask ${UMASK_VALUE}."
        fi
    fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Set default Bash shell file-creation umask to 027 system-wide.
# Idempotent: safe to run multiple times.
#
set -euo pipefail

UMASK_VALUE="027"
TARGET_FILE="/etc/profile.d/set-umask.sh"

# Ensure we are running with sufficient privileges
if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

###############################################################################
# 1. Create/maintain a dedicated profile.d script to enforce the umask for
#    all new interactive login shells. This is the cleanest, idempotent place.
###############################################################################
TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
cat > "${TMP_FILE}" <<EOF
# Set default file-creation mask (umask) for all users.
# Managed by security remediation script. Do not edit manually.
umask ${UMASK_VALUE}
EOF

if [[ ! -f "${TARGET_FILE}" ]] || ! cmp -s "${TMP_FILE}" "${TARGET_FILE}"; then
    install -m 0644 -o root -g root "${TMP_FILE}" "${TARGET_FILE}"
    echo "Updated ${TARGET_FILE} to enforce umask ${UMASK_VALUE}."
else
    echo "${TARGET_FILE} already enforces umask ${UMASK_VALUE}."
fi
rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"

###############################################################################
# 2. Normalize any existing umask declarations in the core shell config files
#    so they do not override the desired value.
###############################################################################
normalize_umask_in_file() {
    local file="$1"

    [[ -f "${file}" ]] || return 0

    # If there is any active (non-commented) umask line that is not 027,
    # replace it with the correct value.
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+' "${file}"; then
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*umask[[:space:]]+0?${UMASK_VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${file}"; then
            sed -ri "s/^([[:space:]]*)umask[[:space:]]+[0-7]+.*$/\1umask ${UMASK_VALUE}/" "${file}"
            echo "Corrected existing umask declaration in ${file}."
        else
            echo "${file} already declares umask ${UMASK_VALUE}."
        fi
    fi
}

normalize_umask_in_file "/etc/profile"
normalize_umask_in_file "/etc/bashrc"

# Handle additional drop-in profile.d files (excluding our managed file)
shopt -s nullglob
for f in /etc/profile.d/*.sh; do
    [[ "${f}" == "${TARGET_FILE}" ]] && continue
    normalize_umask_in_file "${f}"
done
shopt -u nullglob

echo "Default Bash file-creation umask is set to ${UMASK_VALUE} system-wide."
```

set -euo pipefail

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
DROPIN="${RSYSLOG_DIR}/100-cert-name-auth.conf"

DESIRED_AUTHMODE='x509/name'

# Ensure rsyslog is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "rsyslog is not installed; installing..."
    yum install -y rsyslog

if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "rsyslog-gnutls is not installed; installing..."
    yum install -y rsyslog-gnutls
fi

mkdir -p "${RSYSLOG_DIR}"

# Gather all rsyslog configuration files
mapfile -t CONF_FILES < <(
    {
        echo "${RSYSLOG_CONF}"
        find "${RSYSLOG_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' 2>/dev/null
    } | sort -u
)

remediated=0

# 1) Normalize any legacy global directive
#    $ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name
for f in "${CONF_FILES[@]}"; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue

            remediated=1

done

#    global(... DefaultNetstreamDriverAuthMode="x509/name" ...)
#    or action(... StreamDriver.AuthMode="x509/name" ...)

            sed -ri "s|DefaultNetstreamDriverAuthMode\s*=\s*\"[^\"]*\"|DefaultNetstreamDriverAuthMode=\"${DESIRED_AUTHMODE}\"|g" "$f"
            echo "Updated RainerScript DefaultNetstreamDriverAuthMode in $f"

RSYSLOG_DROPIN_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
TLS_DROPIN="${RSYSLOG_DROPIN_DIR}/remote-tls.conf"

REMOTE_PORT="6514"

# TLS material expected to be provisioned on the host.
CA_CERT="/etc/pki/rsyslog/ca.pem"
CLIENT_CERT="/etc/pki/rsyslog/cert.pem"
CLIENT_KEY="/etc/pki/rsyslog/key.pem"

if command -v rpm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Installing rsyslog-gnutls (TLS network stream driver)..."
        if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            dnf install -y rsyslog-gnutls
        elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            yum install -y rsyslog-gnutls
        fi
    fi
fi

    echo "ERROR: rsyslog is not installed; cannot configure encrypted off-loading." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# inadvertently sent unencrypted. Match common forwarding action syntaxes:
#   *.* @host          (UDP, cleartext)
#   *.* @@host         (TCP, cleartext)
#   action(... target=...) without a tls driver
remove_plaintext_forwarding() {
    local f="$1"
    [ -f "$f" ] || return 0

    # Skip the file we manage for TLS.
    [ "$f" = "$TLS_DROPIN" ] && return 0

    # Comment out single-@ (UDP) and double-@@ (TCP) cleartext forwarders.
    if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*[^#].*@{1,2}[^@]' "$f"; then
        sed -ri 's|^([[:space:]]*)([^#].*[[:space:]]@{1,2}[^[:space:]]+.*)$|\1#\2 # disabled: cleartext log forwarding (use TLS)|' "$f"
    fi
}

remove_plaintext_forwarding "$RSYSLOG_CONF"

    for f in "$RSYSLOG_DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf; do
        [ -e "$f" ] || continue
        remove_plaintext_forwarding "$f"

# Managed by remediation script: encrypted (TLS) off-loading of log records.
# Global TLS / x.509 settings for the gtls network stream driver.
\$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls
\$DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile ${CA_CERT}
\$DefaultNetstreamDriverCertFile ${CLIENT_CERT}
\$DefaultNetstreamDriverKeyFile ${CLIENT_KEY}

\$ActionSendStreamDriver gtls
\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1
\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name

# Forward all log records to the remote collector over TLS-protected TCP.
*.* @@${REMOTE_COLLECTOR}:${REMOTE_PORT}
EOF

current_content=""
if [ -f "$TLS_DROPIN" ]; then
    current_content="$(cat "$TLS_DROPIN")"
fi

if [ "$current_content" != "$DESIRED_CONTENT" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$DESIRED_CONTENT" > "$TLS_DROPIN"
    chmod 0600 "$TLS_DROPIN"
    chown root:root "$TLS_DROPIN"
    echo "Wrote TLS forwarding configuration to ${TLS_DROPIN}."
    CONFIG_CHANGED=1
else
    chmod 0600 "$TLS_DROPIN"
    chown root:root "$TLS_DROPIN"
    echo "TLS forwarding configuration already present and correct."
    CONFIG_CHANGED=0
fi

# Validate rsyslog configuration before (re)starting.
if command -v rsyslogd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! rsyslogd -N1 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "WARNING: rsyslog configuration validation reported issues." >&2
    fi
fi

# Ensure the service persists across reboots.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    if [ "${CONFIG_CHANGED}" -eq 1 ] || ! systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then

        systemctl restart rsyslog
    fi

    systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog && \
        echo "rsyslog is active and configured for encrypted log off-loading."
fi

echo "Remediation complete: log off-loading enforced over TLS."

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_DROPIN_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
CUSTOM_CONF="${RSYSLOG_DROPIN_DIR}/99-auth-daemon-logging.conf"

# Ensure rsyslog package is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y rsyslog
    else
        yum install -y rsyslog
    fi
fi

# Ensure drop-in directory exists
mkdir -p "${RSYSLOG_DROPIN_DIR}"

# Ensure the main rsyslog.conf includes the drop-in directory
if [ -f "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" ]; then
    if ! grep -Eq '^\s*\$IncludeConfig\s+/etc/rsyslog\.d/\*\.conf' "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" \
        && ! grep -Eq '^\s*include\(\s*file="/etc/rsyslog\.d/\*\.conf"' "${RSYSLOG_CONF}"; then
        printf '\n$IncludeConfig /etc/rsyslog.d/*.conf\n' >> "${RSYSLOG_CONF}"
    fi
fi

# Desired logging configuration content
read -r -d '' DESIRED_CONTENT <<'EOF' || true
# Managed by remediation script: capture auth, authpriv, and daemon activity
auth.*					/var/log/secure
authpriv.*				/var/log/secure
daemon.*				/var/log/messages
EOF

# Write the drop-in only if it differs (idempotent)
NEED_WRITE=1
if [ -f "${CUSTOM_CONF}" ]; then
    if printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" | diff -q - "${CUSTOM_CONF}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        NEED_WRITE=0
    fi
fi

if [ "${NEED_WRITE}" -eq 1 ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" > "${CUSTOM_CONF}"
fi

# Ensure correct permissions/ownership on the drop-in
chown root:root "${CUSTOM_CONF}"
chmod 0644 "${CUSTOM_CONF}"

# Ensure target log files exist with proper ownership and permissions
for logfile in /var/log/secure /var/log/messages; do
    if [ ! -e "${logfile}" ]; then
        touch "${logfile}"
    fi
    chown root:root "${logfile}"
    chmod 0600 "${logfile}"
done

# Validate rsyslog configuration before applying
if command -v rsyslogd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    rsyslogd -N1
fi

# Ensure rsyslog service is enabled to persist across reboots
systemctl enable rsyslog >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Apply changes by restarting (or starting) rsyslog
if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
    systemctl restart rsyslog
else
    systemctl start rsyslog
fi

echo "Logging configuration for auth, authpriv, and daemon facilities applied successfully."

SYSCTL_PARAMS=(
    "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra"
    "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra"
)

SYSCTL_VALUE="0"
SYSCTL_CONF="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-ipv6-ra.conf"

# Ensure the persistent config file exists
touch "$SYSCTL_CONF"
chmod 0644 "$SYSCTL_CONF"

# Remove any conflicting settings from common sysctl config locations
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "$SYSCTL_CONF" ] && continue
    for param in "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[@]}"; do
        if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param//./\\.}[[:space:]]*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -ri "/^[[:space:]]*${param//./\\.}[[:space:]]*=/d" "$f"
        fi
    done
done

# Write/update the desired parameters idempotently in our dedicated file
for param in "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[@]}"; do
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param//./\\.}[[:space:]]*=" "$SYSCTL_CONF"; then
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${param//./\\.}[[:space:]]*=.*|${param} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}|" "$SYSCTL_CONF"
    else
        echo "${param} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >> "$SYSCTL_CONF"
    fi
done

# Apply at runtime for all current parameters
for param in "${SYSCTL_PARAMS[@]}"; do
    sysctl -w "${param}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null
done

# Apply to every currently existing IPv6 interface at runtime
if [ -d /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf ]; then
    for iface_path in /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/*; do
        iface="$(basename "$iface_path")"
        sysctl -w "net.ipv6.conf.${iface}.accept_ra=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    done
fi

# Reload sysctl settings from persistent configuration
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2

touch "${CONFIG_FILE}"

    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local line="${param} = ${value}"

    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=" "${CONFIG_FILE}"; then
        sed -ri "s|^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=.*|${line}|" "${CONFIG_FILE}"
    else
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${CONFIG_FILE}"
    fi
}

set_persistent_param "${PARAM_DEFAULT}" "${DESIRED_VALUE}"

    [[ -f "${f}" ]] || continue
    [[ "${f}" == "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*(${PARAM_ALL}|${PARAM_DEFAULT})[[:space:]]*=" "${f}" 2>/dev/null; then

    fi
    if grep -Eq "^\s*${PARAM_DEFAULT//./\\.}\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then

${PARAM_DEFAULT} = ${VALUE}

sysctl -w "${PARAM_DEFAULT}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null

PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-icmp-redirects.conf"

# Ensure persistence directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d

# Remove any conflicting settings from existing sysctl config files
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    [ "$f" = "$PERSIST_FILE" ] && continue
    for p in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
        if grep -Eq "^\s*${p//./\\.}\s*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i -E "/^\s*${p//./\\.}\s*=/d" "$f"
        fi
    done
done

# Write idempotent persistent configuration
{
    echo "# Refuse ICMP redirect messages to mitigate MITM/routing-table tampering"
    for p in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
        echo "${p} = 0"
    done
} > "$PERSIST_FILE"

for p in "${PARAMS[@]}"; do
    sysctl -w "${p}=0" >/dev/null

    val="$(sysctl -n "$p")"
    if [ "$val" != "0" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: ${p} is ${val}, expected 0" >&2
        rc=1
    fi
done

exit "$rc"

set -euo pipefail

PARAM="net.ipv4.ip_forward"
VALUE="0"
PERSIST_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-ip-forwarding.conf"
SYSCTL_BIN="/usr/sbin/sysctl"

if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Locate sysctl if not at expected path
if [[ ! -x "$SYSCTL_BIN" ]]; then
    SYSCTL_BIN="$(command -v sysctl || true)"
    if [[ -z "$SYSCTL_BIN" ]]; then
        echo "sysctl command not found." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

#
# 1. Ensure the setting persists across reboots.
#
# Remove any conflicting/duplicate definitions from standard config files
# so our value is authoritative, then write our managed drop-in.
#
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
    # Do not modify our own managed file in this loop
    [[ "$f" == "$PERSIST_FILE" ]] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i -E "/^[[:space:]]*${PARAM//./\\.}[[:space:]]*=/d" "$f"
    fi
done

# Write/refresh the managed drop-in file idempotently
DESIRED_CONTENT="# Managed: disable IPv4 packet forwarding (host is not a router)
${PARAM} = ${VALUE}"

if [[ ! -f "$PERSIST_FILE" ]] || [[ "$(cat "$PERSIST_FILE")" != "$DESIRED_CONTENT" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$DESIRED_CONTENT" > "$PERSIST_FILE"
    chmod 0644 "$PERSIST_FILE"
fi

#
# 2. Apply the setting at runtime.
#
CURRENT_VALUE="$("$SYSCTL_BIN" -n "$PARAM" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
if [[ "$CURRENT_VALUE" != "$VALUE" ]]; then
    "$SYSCTL_BIN" -w "${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null
fi

# Flush the routing cache so the change takes immediate effect.
if [[ -w /proc/sys/net/ipv4/route/flush ]]; then
    echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/route/flush 2>/dev/null || true
fi

#
# 3. Reload sysctl settings from configuration to confirm persistence.
#
"$SYSCTL_BIN" --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

#
# 4. Verify final runtime state.
#
FINAL_VALUE="$("$SYSCTL_BIN" -n "$PARAM" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
if [[ "$FINAL_VALUE" == "$VALUE" ]]; then
    echo "IPv4 packet forwarding is disabled (${PARAM} = ${FINAL_VALUE})."
    exit 0
else
    echo "Failed to disable IPv4 packet forwarding (current value: ${FINAL_VALUE})." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MODULE="atm"
CONF_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/${MODULE}.conf"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d

# Build desired configuration content
INSTALL_LINE="install ${MODULE} /bin/true"
BLACKLIST_LINE="blacklist ${MODULE}"

# Idempotently ensure the install line (blocks on-demand/automatic loading)
if ! grep -Eq "^\s*install\s+${MODULE}\s+/bin/(true|false)\s*$" "${CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "${INSTALL_LINE}" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Idempotently ensure the blacklist line (prevents automatic loading)
if ! grep -Eq "^\s*blacklist\s+${MODULE}\s*$" "${CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "${BLACKLIST_LINE}" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Set safe permissions on the configuration file
chmod 0644 "${CONF_FILE}"
chown root:root "${CONF_FILE}"

# Unload the module if it is currently loaded
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE}\b"; then
    rmmod "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || modprobe -r "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MODULE="tipc"
CONF_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/${MODULE}.conf"

echo "Remediating: disabling ${MODULE} kernel module..."

# Ensure the modprobe configuration directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d
chmod 0755 /etc/modprobe.d

# Create/replace the modprobe configuration to blacklist and disable loading
TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
cat > "${TMP_FILE}" <<EOF
install ${MODULE} /bin/true
blacklist ${MODULE}
EOF

if [ -f "${CONF_FILE}" ] && cmp -s "${TMP_FILE}" "${CONF_FILE}"; then
    echo "Configuration already in place: ${CONF_FILE}"
    rm -f "${TMP_FILE}"
else
    mv "${TMP_FILE}" "${CONF_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${CONF_FILE}"
    echo "Wrote configuration: ${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Unload the module if it is currently loaded
if lsmod | awk '{print $1}' | grep -qx "${MODULE}"; then
    echo "Module ${MODULE} is loaded; attempting to unload..."
    if rmmod "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || modprobe -r "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Module ${MODULE} unloaded."
    else
        echo "Warning: could not unload ${MODULE} (may be in use); it will remain disabled after reboot."
    fi
else
    echo "Module ${MODULE} is not currently loaded."
fi

echo "Remediation complete for ${MODULE}."

if ! grep -Eqs "^\s*install\s+${MODULE}\s+/bin/(true|false)\b" "${CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    if [ -f "${CONF_FILE}" ] && grep -Eqs "^\s*install\s+${MODULE}\b" "${CONF_FILE}"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*install\s+${MODULE}\b.*|install ${MODULE} /bin/false|" "${CONF_FILE}"
    else
        echo "install ${MODULE} /bin/false" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# Ensure the module is blacklisted so it is not automatically loaded
if ! grep -Eqs "^\s*blacklist\s+${MODULE}\s*$" "${CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "blacklist ${MODULE}" >> "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Unload the module if currently loaded
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE}\b"; then
    rmmod "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || modprobe -r "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0

MOUNT_POINT="/dev/shm"

    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Back up fstab before modifying
cp -p "$FSTAB" "$BACKUP"

# Function to check whether an option list contains noexec
has_noexec() {
    local opts="$1"
    [[ ",$opts," == *",noexec,"* ]]
}

# Build a normalized option string ensuring noexec is present and defaults baseline
build_opts() {
    local opts="$1"
    # If empty or "defaults" missing, ensure sensible secure defaults
    if [[ -z "$opts" || "$opts" == "defaults" ]]; then
        echo "defaults,nodev,nosuid,noexec"
        return
    fi
    # Remove any existing exec/noexec tokens then re-add noexec
    local cleaned
    cleaned=$(echo "$opts" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -v -x -E '(no)?exec' | paste -sd, -)
    if [[ -z "$cleaned" ]]; then
        echo "noexec"
    else
        echo "${cleaned},noexec"
    fi
}

found=0

    # Preserve comments and blank lines
    if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] || [[ -z "${line// /}" ]]; then
        echo "$line" >> "$tmpfile"
        continue
    fi

    # Parse fields
    read -r fs_spec fs_file fs_vfstype fs_mntopts fs_dump fs_pass <<< "$line"

    if [[ "$fs_file" == "$MOUNT_POINT" ]]; then
        found=1
        new_opts="$(build_opts "${fs_mntopts:-defaults}")"
        fs_dump="${fs_dump:-0}"
        fs_pass="${fs_pass:-0}"

    else
        echo "$line" >> "$tmpfile"
    fi

if [[ "$found" -eq 0 ]]; then
    printf 'tmpfs\t%s\ttmpfs\tdefaults,nodev,nosuid,noexec\t0 0\n' "$MOUNT_POINT" >> "$tmpfile"
fi

if ! cmp -s "$tmpfile" "$FSTAB"; then
    cat "$tmpfile" > "$FSTAB"
    echo "Updated $FSTAB to enforce noexec on $MOUNT_POINT."
else
    echo "$FSTAB already configured with noexec on $MOUNT_POINT; no changes needed."

if mountpoint -q "$MOUNT_POINT"; then
    current_opts="$(findmnt -no OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if has_noexec "$current_opts"; then
        echo "Active mount $MOUNT_POINT already has noexec."

final_opts="$(findmnt -no OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if has_noexec "$final_opts"; then
    echo "Verification successful: $MOUNT_POINT is mounted with noexec."
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to apply noexec to $MOUNT_POINT." >&2
    exit 1

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: ensure every non-root local filesystem is mounted with "nodev"
# so that no file on it can be interpreted as a character or block device node.
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run repeatedly without adverse effect.

set -u

FSTAB="/etc/fstab"
BACKUP="${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Pseudo / virtual filesystem types that must never appear in our processing.
PSEUDO_FS_RE='^(proc|sysfs|devtmpfs|devpts|tmpfs|cgroup|cgroup2|mqueue|hugetlbfs|debugfs|tracefs|securityfs|pstore|bpf|configfs|fusectl|autofs|binfmt_misc|rpc_pipefs|nfsd|selinuxfs|ramfs|overlay|squashfs|efivarfs|fuse\.gvfsd-fuse|fuse\.portal)$'

# Determine the device backing the root filesystem so we can skip it.
root_src="$(findmnt -no SOURCE / 2>/dev/null)"

echo "Backing up ${FSTAB} to ${BACKUP}"
cp -a -- "${FSTAB}" "${BACKUP}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: decide whether a filesystem type is a "local" type we care about.
# We only want real, on-disk local filesystems (not network or pseudo ones).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
is_local_fs() {
    local fstype="$1"
    case "${fstype}" in
        ext2|ext3|ext4|xfs|btrfs|jfs|reiserfs|vfat|fat|exfat|ntfs|ntfs3|f2fs|minix|udf|iso9660|hfs|hfsplus)
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: add "nodev" to a comma separated mount option string if absent.
# Prints the (possibly modified) option string.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
add_nodev() {
    local opts="$1"
    local IFS=','
    local -a arr
    read -r -a arr <<< "${opts}"
    local o
    for o in "${arr[@]}"; do
        if [ "${o}" = "nodev" ]; then
            echo "${opts}"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    if [ -z "${opts}" ] || [ "${opts}" = "defaults" ]; then
        if [ -z "${opts}" ]; then
            echo "nodev"
        else
            echo "defaults,nodev"
        fi
    else
        echo "${opts},nodev"
    fi
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Process /etc/fstab, rewriting each qualifying entry to include nodev.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TMP="$(mktemp)"

while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "${line}" ]; do
    # Preserve blank lines and comments unchanged.
    case "${line}" in
        ''|\#*)
            printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Parse the six standard fstab fields.
    read -r f_dev f_mnt f_type f_opts f_dump f_pass _rest <<< "${line}"

    # If the line is malformed (missing fields), leave it untouched.
    if [ -z "${f_dev}" ] || [ -z "${f_mnt}" ] || [ -z "${f_type}" ] || [ -z "${f_opts}" ]; then
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
        continue
    fi

    # Skip the root mount point.
    if [ "${f_mnt}" = "/" ]; then
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
        continue
    fi

    # Skip swap and pseudo/virtual filesystem types.
    if [ "${f_type}" = "swap" ] || printf '%s' "${f_type}" | grep -Eq "${PSEUDO_FS_RE}"; then
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
        continue
    fi

    # Only act on genuine local on-disk filesystem types.
    if ! is_local_fs "${f_type}"; then
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
        continue
    fi

    new_opts="$(add_nodev "${f_opts}")"

    # Set sane defaults for dump/pass if they were missing.
    [ -z "${f_dump}" ] && f_dump="0"
    [ -z "${f_pass}" ] && f_pass="0"

    if [ "${new_opts}" != "${f_opts}" ]; then
        echo "Adding 'nodev' to fstab entry for ${f_mnt} (${f_dev})"
    fi

    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' \
        "${f_dev}" "${f_mnt}" "${f_type}" "${new_opts}" "${f_dump}" "${f_pass}" >> "${TMP}"

done < "${FSTAB}"

# Replace fstab only if something actually changed.
if cmp -s "${TMP}" "${FSTAB}"; then
    echo "No changes required in ${FSTAB}."
    rm -f "${BACKUP}"
else
    cat "${TMP}" > "${FSTAB}"
    echo "Updated ${FSTAB}."
fi
rm -f "${TMP}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Apply the nodev option to currently mounted non-root local filesystems
# without requiring a reboot, where possible.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
while read -r target source fstype options; do
    [ "${target}" = "/" ] && continue
    [ "${fstype}" = "swap" ] && continue

    if [ -n "${root_src}" ] && [ "${source}" = "${root_src}" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    if printf '%s' "${fstype}" | grep -Eq "${PSEUDO_FS_RE}"; then
        continue
    fi

    if ! is_local_fs "${fstype}"; then
        continue
    fi

    # Already has nodev?
    if printf ',%s,' "${options}" | grep -q ',nodev,'; then
        continue
    fi

    echo "Remounting ${target} with nodev"
    if ! mount -o remount,nodev "${target}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Warning: could not remount ${target} with nodev now; change will apply on next mount/reboot." >&2
    fi
done < <(findmnt -rn -o TARGET,SOURCE,FSTYPE,OPTIONS)

echo "Remediation complete."

#
# Remediation: ensure every non-root local filesystem is mounted with "nodev"
# so that no file on it can be interpreted as a character or block device node.
#
# This script is idempotent: it can be run repeatedly without adverse effect.

set -u

FSTAB="/etc/fstab"
BACKUP="${FSTAB}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Pseudo / virtual filesystem types that must never appear in our processing.
PSEUDO_FS_RE='^(proc|sysfs|devtmpfs|devpts|tmpfs|cgroup|cgroup2|mqueue|hugetlbfs|debugfs|tracefs|securityfs|pstore|bpf|configfs|fusectl|autofs|binfmt_misc|rpc_pipefs|nfsd|selinuxfs|ramfs|overlay|squashfs|efivarfs|fuse\.gvfsd-fuse|fuse\.portal)$'

# Determine the device backing the root filesystem so we can skip it.
root_src="$(findmnt -no SOURCE / 2>/dev/null)"

echo "Backing up ${FSTAB} to ${BACKUP}"
cp -a -- "${FSTAB}" "${BACKUP}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: decide whether a filesystem type is a "local" type we care about.
# We only want real, on-disk local filesystems (not network or pseudo ones).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
is_local_fs() {
    local fstype="$1"
    case "${fstype}" in
        ext2|ext3|ext4|xfs|btrfs|jfs|reiserfs|vfat|fat|exfat|ntfs|ntfs3|f2fs|minix|udf|iso9660|hfs|hfsplus)
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: add "nodev" to a comma separated mount option string if absent.
# Prints the (possibly modified) option string.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
add_nodev() {
    local opts="$1"
    local IFS=','
    local -a arr
    read -r -a arr <<< "${opts}"
    local o
    for o in "${arr[@]}"; do
        if [ "${o}" = "nodev" ]; then
            echo "${opts}"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    if [ -z "${opts}" ] || [ "${opts}" = "defaults" ]; then
        if [ -z "${opts}" ]; then
            echo "nodev"
        else
            echo "defaults,nodev"
        fi
    else
        echo "${opts},nodev"
    fi
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Process /etc/fstab, rewriting each qualifying entry to include nodev.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TMP="$(mktemp)"

while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "${line}" ]; do
    # Preserve blank lines and comments unchanged.
    case "${line}" in
        ''|\#*)
            printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
            continue
            ;;
    esac

    # Parse the six standard fstab fields.
    read -r f_dev f_mnt f_type f_opts f_dump f_pass _rest <<< "${line}"

    # If the line is malformed (missing fields), leave it untouched.
    if [ -z "${f_dev}" ] || [ -z "${f_mnt}" ] || [ -z "${f_type}" ] || [ -z "${f_opts}" ]; then
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
        continue
    fi

    # Skip the root mount point.
    if [ "${f_mnt}" = "/" ]; then
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
        continue
    fi

    # Skip swap and pseudo/virtual filesystem types.
    if [ "${f_type}" = "swap" ] || printf '%s' "${f_type}" | grep -Eq "${PSEUDO_FS_RE}"; then
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
        continue
    fi

    # Only act on genuine local on-disk filesystem types.
    if ! is_local_fs "${f_type}"; then
        printf '%s\n' "${line}" >> "${TMP}"
        continue
    fi

    new_opts="$(add_nodev "${f_opts}")"

    # Set sane defaults for dump/pass if they were missing.
    [ -z "${f_dump}" ] && f_dump="0"
    [ -z "${f_pass}" ] && f_pass="0"

    if [ "${new_opts}" != "${f_opts}" ]; then
        echo "Adding 'nodev' to fstab entry for ${f_mnt} (${f_dev})"
    fi

    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n' \
        "${f_dev}" "${f_mnt}" "${f_type}" "${new_opts}" "${f_dump}" "${f_pass}" >> "${TMP}"

done < "${FSTAB}"

# Replace fstab only if something actually changed.
if cmp -s "${TMP}" "${FSTAB}"; then
    echo "No changes required in ${FSTAB}."
    rm -f "${BACKUP}"
else
    cat "${TMP}" > "${FSTAB}"
    echo "Updated ${FSTAB}."
fi
rm -f "${TMP}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Apply the nodev option to currently mounted non-root local filesystems
# without requiring a reboot, where possible.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
while read -r target source fstype options; do
    [ "${target}" = "/" ] && continue
    [ "${fstype}" = "swap" ] && continue

    if [ -n "${root_src}" ] && [ "${source}" = "${root_src}" ]; then
        continue
    fi

    if printf '%s' "${fstype}" | grep -Eq "${PSEUDO_FS_RE}"; then
        continue
    fi

    if ! is_local_fs "${fstype}"; then
        continue
    fi

    # Already has nodev?
    if printf ',%s,' "${options}" | grep -q ',nodev,'; then
        continue
    fi

    echo "Remounting ${target} with nodev"
    if ! mount -o remount,nodev "${target}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Warning: could not remount ${target} with nodev now; change will apply on next mount/reboot." >&2
    fi
done < <(findmnt -rn -o TARGET,SOURCE,FSTYPE,OPTIONS)

echo "Remediation complete."
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MODULE="uvcvideo"
CONF_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/${MODULE}.conf"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d

# Build the desired configuration content
DESIRED_CONTENT="install ${MODULE} /bin/false
blacklist ${MODULE}"

# Write the configuration idempotently
if [[ ! -f "${CONF_FILE}" ]] || [[ "$(cat "${CONF_FILE}")" != "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" > "${CONF_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Unload the module if it is currently loaded
if lsmod | grep -q "^${MODULE}\b"; then
    modprobe -r "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || rmmod "${MODULE}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

EOF

#
# Remediation: Restrict access to the kernel message (ring) buffer
# by enforcing kernel.dmesg_restrict = 1.
# This prevents unprivileged users from reading the kernel ring buffer.
#
set -euo pipefail

PARAM="kernel.dmesg_restrict"
VALUE="1"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/sysctl.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/99-dmesg-restrict.conf"

# Ensure we are running with sufficient privileges.
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the sysctl drop-in directory exists.
mkdir -p "${DROPIN_DIR}"

# Write/refresh the persistent configuration idempotently.
desired_line="${PARAM} = ${VALUE}"
if [[ -f "${DROPIN_FILE}" ]] && grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${DROPIN_FILE}"; then
    echo "Persistent setting already present in ${DROPIN_FILE}."
else
    printf '# Restrict access to the kernel message buffer\n%s\n' "${desired_line}" > "${DROPIN_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${DROPIN_FILE}"
    echo "Wrote persistent setting to ${DROPIN_FILE}."
fi

# Remove any conflicting definitions in other common sysctl config files.
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [[ -e "${f}" ]] || continue
    [[ "${f}" == "${DROPIN_FILE}" ]] && continue
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "${f}"; then
        if ! grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${f}"; then
            sed -ri "/^[[:space:]]*${PARAM}[[:space:]]*=/d" "${f}"
            echo "Removed conflicting ${PARAM} entry from ${f}."
        fi
    fi
done

# Apply the setting immediately at runtime.
current="$(sysctl -n "${PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
if [[ "${current}" != "${VALUE}" ]]; then
    sysctl -w "${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null
    echo "Applied ${PARAM}=${VALUE} at runtime."
else
    echo "Runtime value already ${PARAM}=${VALUE}."

# Reload from the drop-in file to confirm persistence config is valid.
sysctl -p "${DROPIN_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Verify final state.
final="$(sysctl -n "${PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
if [[ "${final}" == "${VALUE}" ]]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${PARAM} is set to ${final}."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to set ${PARAM} (current: '${final}')." >&2
    exit 1

CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-perf-event-paranoid.conf"

# Apply the setting immediately at runtime
sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_KEY}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null

# Remove the setting from any other sysctl config files to avoid conflicts
for f in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
    [ -f "$f" ] || continue
    if [ "$f" != "$CONF_FILE" ] && grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_KEY}\b" "$f"; then
        sed -i -E "/^\s*${SYSCTL_KEY}\b/d" "$f"
    fi
done

# Ensure the persistent configuration file contains exactly the desired setting
if [ ! -f "$CONF_FILE" ] || ! grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_KEY}\s*=\s*${SYSCTL_VALUE}\s*$" "$CONF_FILE"; then
    if [ -f "$CONF_FILE" ] && grep -Eq "^\s*${SYSCTL_KEY}\b" "$CONF_FILE"; then
        sed -i -E "s|^\s*${SYSCTL_KEY}\b.*|${SYSCTL_KEY} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}|" "$CONF_FILE"
    else
        echo "${SYSCTL_KEY} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >> "$CONF_FILE"
    fi
fi

# Reload sysctl settings to confirm persistence configuration is valid
sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Re-apply to ensure runtime value is correct after reload
sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_KEY}=${SYSCTL_VALUE}" >/dev/null

current="$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_KEY}")"
if [ "$current" -eq "$SYSCTL_VALUE" ]; then
    echo "Successfully set ${SYSCTL_KEY} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}"
else
    echo "Failed to set ${SYSCTL_KEY} (current: ${current})" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

UNIT="systemd-coredump.socket"
SERVICE="systemd-coredump.service"
CONF_DIR="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf.d"
CONF_FILE="${CONF_DIR}/99-disable-coredump.conf"
SYSCTL_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-coredump.conf"
LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.d/99-coredump.conf"

if ! command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "systemctl not found; this script requires systemd." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Stop the socket if it is active
if systemctl is-active --quiet "${UNIT}" 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl stop "${UNIT}" || true
fi

# Stop the service if it is active
if systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl stop "${SERVICE}" || true
fi

# Mask the socket so it cannot be started or activated
if [ "$(systemctl is-enabled "${UNIT}" 2>/dev/null || true)" != "masked" ]; then
    systemctl mask "${UNIT}" || true
fi

# Mask the service so it cannot be started or activated
if [ "$(systemctl is-enabled "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null || true)" != "masked" ]; then
    systemctl mask "${SERVICE}" || true
fi

# Configure systemd coredump handler to store nothing as defense in depth
mkdir -p "${CONF_DIR}"
DESIRED_CONF=$'[Coredump]\nStorage=none\nProcessSizeMax=0\n'
if [ ! -f "${CONF_FILE}" ] || [ "$(cat "${CONF_FILE}")" != "${DESIRED_CONF}" ]; then
    printf '%s' "${DESIRED_CONF}" > "${CONF_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${CONF_FILE}"
fi

# Disable core dumps via sysctl
DESIRED_SYSCTL=$'kernel.core_pattern=|/bin/false\nfs.suid_dumpable=0\n'
if [ ! -f "${SYSCTL_FILE}" ] || [ "$(cat "${SYSCTL_FILE}")" != "${DESIRED_SYSCTL}" ]; then
    printf '%s' "${DESIRED_SYSCTL}" > "${SYSCTL_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${SYSCTL_FILE}"
fi

# Apply sysctl settings now
sysctl -w kernel.core_pattern="|/bin/false" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
sysctl -w fs.suid_dumpable=0 >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

# Enforce a hard limit of zero on core dump size for all users
DESIRED_LIMITS=$'* hard core 0\n* soft core 0\nroot hard core 0\nroot soft core 0\n'
if [ ! -f "${LIMITS_FILE}" ] || [ "$(cat "${LIMITS_FILE}")" != "${DESIRED_LIMITS}" ]; then
    printf '%s' "${DESIRED_LIMITS}" > "${LIMITS_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${LIMITS_FILE}"
fi

# Reload systemd to pick up masking and config changes
systemctl daemon-reload || true

echo "Core dump handling has been disabled and masked."

PARAM="kernel.randomize_va_space"

DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/60-kernel-aslr.conf"

    chmod 0644 "${DROPIN_FILE}"
fi

sysctl -w "${PARAM}=${VALUE}" >/dev/null

if command -v sysctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    sysctl --system >/dev/null 2>&1 || sysctl -p "${DROPIN_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

    echo "ERROR: Failed to set ${PARAM} to ${VALUE} (current: ${CURRENT})" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

PARAM="page_poison=1"

# Apply to all currently installed kernels (existing boot entries)
grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="${PARAM}"

# Ensure the parameter is applied to future kernels via default cmdline
if [ -f /etc/default/grub ]; then
    if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' /etc/default/grub; then
        current=$(grep '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' /etc/default/grub | sed -e 's/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="//' -e 's/"$//')
        if ! grep -qw "page_poison=1" <<< "$current"; then
            # remove any existing page_poison setting to avoid duplicates
            newval=$(echo "$current" | sed -E 's/\bpage_poison=[^ ]*//g' | tr -s ' ' | sed -e 's/^ //' -e 's/ $//')
            if [ -n "$newval" ]; then
                newval="${newval} ${PARAM}"
            else
                newval="${PARAM}"
            fi
            sed -i "s|^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${newval}\"|" /etc/default/grub
        fi
    else
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}\"" >> /etc/default/grub
    fi
else
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}\"" > /etc/default/grub
fi

# Regenerate grub configuration so future kernels inherit the setting
if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg"
else
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"

        grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg

        grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg

PARAM="slub_debug=P"

if command -v grubby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Remove any existing slub_debug to avoid conflicting values, then add ours
    for kernel in $(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | awk -F= '/^kernel=/{gsub(/"/,"",$2); print $2}'); do
        current=$(grubby --info="$kernel" 2>/dev/null | sed -n 's/^args="\(.*\)"$/\1/p')
        if echo "$current" | grep -qw "slub_debug=P"; then
            :
        else
            grubby --update-kernel="$kernel" --remove-args="slub_debug" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
            grubby --update-kernel="$kernel" --args="$PARAM" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        fi

    # Update the default/template used for future kernels
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --remove-args="slub_debug" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="$PARAM" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

    if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE"; then
        line=$(grep '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE")

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SERVICE="kdump.service"

# Ensure systemctl is available
if ! command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "systemctl not found; cannot manage services." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Stop the service if it is currently active
if systemctl is-active --quiet "$SERVICE"; then
    echo "Stopping ${SERVICE}..."
    systemctl stop "$SERVICE"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already stopped."
fi

# Disable the service so it does not start at boot
if systemctl is-enabled --quiet "$SERVICE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Disabling ${SERVICE}..."
    systemctl disable "$SERVICE"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already disabled."
fi

# Mask the service to prevent manual or dependency-based activation

if [ "$MASK_STATE" != "masked" ]; then
    echo "Masking ${SERVICE}..."
    systemctl mask "$SERVICE"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already masked."
fi

echo "Final state of ${SERVICE}:"
systemctl is-active "$SERVICE" || true
systemctl is-enabled "$SERVICE" || true

set -euo pipefail

SERVICE="fapolicyd"

    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        dnf install -y "${SERVICE}"
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        yum install -y "${SERVICE}"
    else
        echo "ERROR: No supported package manager found to install ${SERVICE}." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Reload systemd in case the unit was just installed.
systemctl daemon-reload || true

# Enable the service so it starts automatically at boot (idempotent).
if ! systemctl is-enabled --quiet "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl enable "${SERVICE}"
fi

# Start the service if it is not already active (idempotent).
if ! systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE}" 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl start "${SERVICE}"
fi

# Verify final state.
systemctl is-enabled --quiet "${SERVICE}" && systemctl is-active --quiet "${SERVICE}"
echo "${SERVICE} is enabled and running."

fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Configure chrony to operate in client-only mode on RHEL 8.
# Ensures chrony never opens a listening network port for serving time.
# This is achieved by setting "port 0" and "cmdport 0" in the chrony config.
# The script is idempotent and persists across reboots.

set -euo pipefail

CHRONY_CONF="/etc/chrony.conf"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/chrony.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/client-only.conf"

# Verify chrony configuration file exists; if not, ensure base config presence.
if [[ ! -f "${CHRONY_CONF}" ]]; then
    echo "chrony configuration file ${CHRONY_CONF} not found. Is chrony installed?" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Determine whether the main chrony.conf sources the drop-in directory.
USE_DROPIN=0
if grep -Eq '^[[:space:]]*(include|sourcedir)[[:space:]]+/etc/chrony\.d' "${CHRONY_CONF}"; then
    USE_DROPIN=1
fi

# Helper: ensure a directive with a specific value is set in a given file.
# Removes/comments conflicting existing entries and appends the desired value.
enforce_directive() {
    local file="$1"
    local key="$2"
    local value="$3"
    local desired="${key} ${value}"

    # Ensure file exists.
    [[ -f "${file}" ]] || touch "${file}"

    # If the exact desired line already exists (uncommented), nothing to do.
    if grep -Eq "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+${value}([[:space:]]*(#.*)?)?$" "${file}"; then
        # Still ensure no other conflicting lines for the same key exist.
        if [[ "$(grep -Ec "^[[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+" "${file}")" -gt 1 ]]; then
            # Comment out all, then re-add one canonical line.
            sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+.*)$/# \1/" "${file}"
            echo "${desired}" >> "${file}"
        fi
        return 0
    fi

    # Comment out any existing (active) directives for this key.
    sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*${key}[[:space:]]+.*)$/# \1/" "${file}"

    # Append the desired directive.
    echo "${desired}" >> "${file}"
}

if [[ "${USE_DROPIN}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    # Use a drop-in file for clean, override-style configuration.
    mkdir -p "${DROPIN_DIR}"

    # Neutralize any conflicting directives in the main config.
    sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*port[[:space:]]+.*)$/# \1/" "${CHRONY_CONF}"
    sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*cmdport[[:space:]]+.*)$/# \1/" "${CHRONY_CONF}"
    # Disable any time-serving directives that could open access.
    sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*(allow|bindaddress|bindcmdaddress)[[:space:]]+.*)$/# \1/" "${CHRONY_CONF}"

    {
        echo "# Managed by remediation: enforce chrony client-only mode."
        echo "# port 0 disables the NTP server socket."
        echo "# cmdport 0 disables the command/monitoring socket."
        echo "port 0"
        echo "cmdport 0"
    } > "${DROPIN_FILE}.tmp"

    # Only overwrite if content differs (idempotency, preserve mtime when unchanged).
    if [[ ! -f "${DROPIN_FILE}" ]] || ! cmp -s "${DROPIN_FILE}.tmp" "${DROPIN_FILE}"; then
        mv "${DROPIN_FILE}.tmp" "${DROPIN_FILE}"
    else
        rm -f "${DROPIN_FILE}.tmp"
    fi

    chmod 0644 "${DROPIN_FILE}"
    chown root:root "${DROPIN_FILE}"
else
    # Apply directives directly to the main chrony.conf.
    enforce_directive "${CHRONY_CONF}" "port" "0"
    enforce_directive "${CHRONY_CONF}" "cmdport" "0"

    # Comment out time-serving / binding directives that expand attack surface.
    sed -i -E "s/^([[:space:]]*(allow|bindaddress|bindcmdaddress)[[:space:]]+.*)$/# \1/" "${CHRONY_CONF}"
fi

# Restart chrony to apply the configuration if the service is managed by systemd.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl list-unit-files 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^chronyd\.service'; then
        systemctl enable chronyd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        if systemctl is-active --quiet chronyd; then
            systemctl restart chronyd
        else
            systemctl start chronyd || true
        fi
    fi
fi

echo "chrony has been configured for client-only mode (port 0, cmdport 0)."
