#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure each chrony remote time source has an explicit maxpoll
# defined so that upstream time sources are polled at a bounded interval.
#
set -euo pipefail

# Maximum poll interval exponent (2^10 = 1024 seconds ~ 17 minutes).
MAXPOLL_VALUE="${MAXPOLL_VALUE:-10}"

# Candidate chrony configuration files/locations.
declare -a CONFIG_FILES=()

if [ -f /etc/chrony.conf ]; then
    CONFIG_FILES+=("/etc/chrony.conf")
fi

if [ -d /etc/chrony.d ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        CONFIG_FILES+=("$f")
    done < <(find /etc/chrony.d -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0)
fi

if [ "${#CONFIG_FILES[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "No chrony configuration files found. Nothing to remediate." >&2
    exit 0
fi

changed=0

# Process each config file, adding/fixing maxpoll on server/pool/peer lines.
for cfg in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}"; do
    [ -f "$cfg" ] || continue

    tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
    file_changed=0

    while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "$line" ]; do
        # Identify the leading directive keyword (ignoring leading whitespace).
        keyword="$(printf '%s\n' "$line" | sed -n 's/^[[:space:]]*\([^[:space:]#]*\).*/\1/p')"

        case "$keyword" in
            server|pool|peer)
                if printf '%s\n' "$line" | grep -Eq '(^|[[:space:]])maxpoll([[:space:]]|$)'; then
                    # maxpoll already present: normalize its value.
                    newline="$(printf '%s\n' "$line" | \
                        sed -E "s/(^|[[:space:]])maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+/\1maxpoll ${MAXPOLL_VALUE}/")"
                else
                    # Append maxpoll to the directive line.
                    newline="$(printf '%s' "$line" | sed -E 's/[[:space:]]+$//')"
                    newline="${newline} maxpoll ${MAXPOLL_VALUE}"
                fi

                if [ "$newline" != "$line" ]; then
                    file_changed=1
                fi
                printf '%s\n' "$newline" >> "$tmpfile"
                ;;
            *)
                printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmpfile"
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$cfg"

    if [ "$file_changed" -eq 1 ]; then
        cat "$tmpfile" > "$cfg"
        echo "Updated maxpoll settings in: $cfg"
        changed=1
    fi
    rm -f "$tmpfile"
done

# Restart chrony service if changes were made so they take effect.
if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^chronyd\.service'; then
            systemctl restart chronyd || \
                echo "Warning: failed to restart chronyd; please restart manually." >&2
        fi

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure each chrony remote time source has an explicit maxpoll
# defined so that upstream time sources are polled at a bounded interval.
#
set -euo pipefail

# Maximum poll interval exponent (2^10 = 1024 seconds ~ 17 minutes).
MAXPOLL_VALUE="${MAXPOLL_VALUE:-10}"

# Candidate chrony configuration files/locations.
declare -a CONFIG_FILES=()

if [ -f /etc/chrony.conf ]; then
    CONFIG_FILES+=("/etc/chrony.conf")
fi

if [ -d /etc/chrony.d ]; then
    while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
        CONFIG_FILES+=("$f")
    done < <(find /etc/chrony.d -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.conf' -print0)
fi

if [ "${#CONFIG_FILES[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "No chrony configuration files found. Nothing to remediate." >&2
    exit 0
fi

changed=0

# Process each config file, adding/fixing maxpoll on server/pool/peer lines.
for cfg in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}"; do
    [ -f "$cfg" ] || continue

    tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
    file_changed=0

    while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "$line" ]; do
        # Identify the leading directive keyword (ignoring leading whitespace).
        keyword="$(printf '%s\n' "$line" | sed -n 's/^[[:space:]]*\([^[:space:]#]*\).*/\1/p')"

        case "$keyword" in
            server|pool|peer)
                if printf '%s\n' "$line" | grep -Eq '(^|[[:space:]])maxpoll([[:space:]]|$)'; then
                    # maxpoll already present: normalize its value.
                    newline="$(printf '%s\n' "$line" | \
                        sed -E "s/(^|[[:space:]])maxpoll[[:space:]]+[0-9]+/\1maxpoll ${MAXPOLL_VALUE}/")"
                else
                    # Append maxpoll to the directive line.
                    newline="$(printf '%s' "$line" | sed -E 's/[[:space:]]+$//')"
                    newline="${newline} maxpoll ${MAXPOLL_VALUE}"
                fi

                if [ "$newline" != "$line" ]; then
                    file_changed=1
                fi
                printf '%s\n' "$newline" >> "$tmpfile"
                ;;
            *)
                printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$tmpfile"
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$cfg"

    if [ "$file_changed" -eq 1 ]; then
        cat "$tmpfile" > "$cfg"
        echo "Updated maxpoll settings in: $cfg"
        changed=1
    fi
    rm -f "$tmpfile"
done

# Restart chrony service if changes were made so they take effect.
if [ "$changed" -eq 1 ]; then
    if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^chronyd\.service'; then
            systemctl restart chronyd || \
                echo "Warning: failed to restart chronyd; please restart manually." >&2
        fi
    fi
    echo "Remediation applied: explicit maxpoll=${MAXPOLL_VALUE} ensured for all time sources."
else
    echo "All time sources already have a bounded maxpoll. No changes needed."
fi

exit 0
```

# Ensure the config file exists
if [[ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG does not exist." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Remove any existing X11Forwarding directives (commented or active) that are
# not within a Match block context handling — we handle the global setting.
# First, comment out any existing active or commented X11Forwarding lines
# that appear before any Match block, then append a single authoritative line.

# Create a temporary file
TMPFILE="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$TMPFILE"' EXIT

in_match_block=0
setting_written=0

while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
    # Detect Match block start (lines beginning with "Match")
    if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*Match[[:space:]] ]]; then
        in_match_block=1
        printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$TMPFILE"
        continue
    fi

    # Outside Match blocks, handle X11Forwarding directives
    if [[ $in_match_block -eq 0 ]] && \
       [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*X11Forwarding([[:space:]]|$) ]]; then
        if [[ $setting_written -eq 0 ]]; then
            printf '%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$TMPFILE"
            setting_written=1
        fi
        continue
    fi

    printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$TMPFILE"
done < "$SSHD_CONFIG"

# If no X11Forwarding directive was found in the global section, append one
if [[ $setting_written -eq 0 ]]; then
    printf '%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$TMPFILE"
fi

# Only replace the file if content has changed (idempotency)
if ! cmp -s "$TMPFILE" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    # Preserve permissions and ownership
    chmod --reference="$SSHD_CONFIG" "$TMPFILE" 2>/dev/null || chmod 0600 "$TMPFILE"
    chown --reference="$SSHD_CONFIG" "$TMPFILE" 2>/dev/null || true
    cp -f "$TMPFILE" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    echo "Updated $SSHD_CONFIG: set $PARAM $VALUE"

    # Validate configuration before restarting
    if sshd -t; then
        if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd 2>/dev/null || \
           systemctl is-enabled --quiet sshd 2>/dev/null; then
            systemctl restart sshd
            echo "Restarted sshd service."
        fi
    else
        echo "Error: sshd configuration test failed. Not restarting." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
else
    echo "$SSHD_CONFIG already configured with $PARAM $VALUE. No changes made."
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Enforce SSH StrictModes so that sshd verifies ownership and
# permissions of the user's home directory and SSH-related files before
# accepting a login.
#
set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
DIRECTIVE="StrictModes"
VALUE="yes"

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}" ]]; then
    echo "Error: ${SSHD_CONFIG} not found." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Back up the original config once.
if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.orig" ]]; then
    cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.orig"
fi

# Remove any existing (active or commented) StrictModes lines, then append the
# enforced directive. This makes the operation idempotent.
tmpfile="$(mktemp)"
grep -viE '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*StrictModes([[:space:]]|$)' "${SSHD_CONFIG}" > "${tmpfile}"
printf '%s %s\n' "${DIRECTIVE}" "${VALUE}" >> "${tmpfile}"

# Only replace the file if content actually changed.
if ! cmp -s "${tmpfile}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"; then
    cat "${tmpfile}" > "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    chmod 0600 "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
    changed=1
else
    changed=0
fi
rm -f "${tmpfile}"

# Validate the resulting sshd configuration.
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if ! sshd -t; then
        echo "Error: sshd configuration test failed. Restoring backup." >&2
        cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}.orig" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Persist across reboots by ensuring the service is enabled and reloading it
# so the new setting takes effect immediately.
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    systemctl enable sshd >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    if systemctl is-active sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if [[ "${changed}" -eq 1 ]]; then
            systemctl reload sshd 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart sshd
        fi
    fi
fi

echo "StrictModes is set to '${VALUE}' in ${SSHD_CONFIG}."
exit 0

    cat > "${BANNER_FILE}" <<'EOF'
WARNING

This system is for the use of authorized users only. Individuals using this

subject to having all of their activities on this system monitored and

In the course of monitoring individuals improperly using this system, or in

Anyone using this system expressly consents to such monitoring and is

activity, system personnel may provide the evidence of such monitoring to
law enforcement officials.
EOF

chown root:root "${BANNER_FILE}"

    sed -ri "s|^\s*Banner\s+.*|Banner ${BANNER_FILE}|" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

    if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then

    else
        systemctl restart sshd
    fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
PARAM="PrintLastLog"
VALUE="yes"

# Ensure the config file exists
if [[ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG does not exist." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Remove any existing (commented or uncommented) PrintLastLog directives
# that are not already the correct setting, then ensure the correct one is present.
if grep -Eiq '^\s*PrintLastLog\b' "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    # Replace the first active occurrence and comment out any others
    sed -ri "s|^\s*#?\s*PrintLastLog\b.*|${PARAM} ${VALUE}|I" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    # Collapse duplicates: keep first matching line, remove subsequent ones
    awk -v param="$PARAM" '
        BEGIN { seen = 0 }
        {
            if ($0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*"param"[[:space:]]") {
                if (seen) next
                seen = 1
            }
            print
        }
    ' "$SSHD_CONFIG" > "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
    cat "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp" > "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    rm -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.tmp"
else
    printf '%s %s\n' "$PARAM" "$VALUE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

# Validate the configuration
if command -v sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    sshd -t
fi

# Reload sshd so the change takes effect for subsequent logins
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
        systemctl reload sshd || systemctl restart sshd
    fi
fi

echo "PrintLastLog is set to '${VALUE}' in ${SSHD_CONFIG}."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Seed the OpenSSH server's RNG from a high-quality entropy
# source using 32 bytes of entropy, persistently and idempotently.
#
# OpenSSH (and the underlying OpenSSL crypto library) honors the
# SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG environment variable. When set to a positive integer N,
# the RNG is seeded with N bytes (N>=14 enforced, here 32) read from
# /dev/random (a high-quality, blocking entropy source) before use.
#
set -euo pipefail

readonly ENV_FILE="/etc/sysconfig/sshd"
readonly KEY="SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG"
readonly VALUE="32"
readonly DESIRED="${KEY}=${VALUE}"

# Must run as root to modify system configuration and restart the service.
if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

changed=0

# Ensure the sysconfig file exists.
if [[ ! -f "${ENV_FILE}" ]]; then
    echo "Creating ${ENV_FILE}"
    touch "${ENV_FILE}"
    chmod 0644 "${ENV_FILE}"
    chown root:root "${ENV_FILE}"
    changed=1
fi

# Determine current state.
current_line="$(grep -E '^[[:space:]]*SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=' "${ENV_FILE}" || true)"

if [[ "${current_line}" == "${DESIRED}" ]]; then
    echo "Setting already correct: ${DESIRED}"
else
    # Remove any existing (commented or active) definitions to avoid duplicates.
    if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=' "${ENV_FILE}"; then
        sed -i -E '/^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=/d' "${ENV_FILE}"
    fi

    # Append the desired setting.
    printf '%s\n' "${DESIRED}" >> "${ENV_FILE}"
    echo "Set ${DESIRED} in ${ENV_FILE}"
    changed=1
fi

# Ensure correct permissions regardless.
chmod 0644 "${ENV_FILE}"
chown root:root "${ENV_FILE}"

# Restart sshd so the new environment takes effect, only if something changed.
if [[ "${changed}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if systemctl is-enabled sshd >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl is-active sshd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Restarting sshd to apply changes..."
            systemctl restart sshd || {
                echo "WARNING: Failed to restart sshd; please restart manually." >&2
            }
        else
            echo "sshd is not active/enabled; configuration applied for next start."
        fi
    else
        echo "systemctl not available; please restart sshd manually."
    fi
else
    echo "No changes required; sshd not restarted."
fi

# Verify final state.
if grep -qxE "${DESIRED}" "${ENV_FILE}"; then
    echo "Verification passed: ${DESIRED} is present in ${ENV_FILE}"
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Verification failed; ${DESIRED} not found in ${ENV_FILE}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

REQUIRED_PKGS=(sssd sssd-tools authselect opensc pcsc-lite pcsc-lite-ccid)

    if ! rpm -q "$pkg" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        PKGS_TO_INSTALL+=("$pkg")
    fi
done

if [ "${#PKGS_TO_INSTALL[@]}" -gt 0 ]; then

    yum install -y "${PKGS_TO_INSTALL[@]}"

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------

if [ ! -f "$SSSD_CONF" ]; then
    cat > "$SSSD_CONF" <<'EOF'
[sssd]
services = nss, pam
domains = local

[pam]

[domain/local]
id_provider = files
EOF
fi

# Idempotent: updates existing key, adds it if missing, creates section.
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------
set_ini_kv() {

        printf '\n[%s]\n%s = %s\n' "$section" "$key" "$value" >> "$file"
        return
    fi

    # Use awk to operate within the target section.
    local tmp
    tmp="$(mktemp)"
    awk -v sect="[$section]" -v key="$key" -v val="$value" '
        BEGIN { in_sect=0; found=0 }
        /^[[:space:]]*\[.*\][[:space:]]*$/ {
            # When leaving target section without finding key, append it.
            if (in_sect == 1 && found == 0) {
                print key " = " val
                found=1
            }
            # Determine if we are entering the target section
            line=$0
            gsub(/^[[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]+$/, "", line)
            if (line == sect) { in_sect=1 } else { in_sect=0 }
            print $0
            next
        }
        {
            if (in_sect == 1 && $0 ~ "^[[:space:]]*" key "[[:space:]]*=") {
                if (found == 0) {
                    print key " = " val
                    found=1
                }
                next
            }
            print $0
        }
        END {
            if (in_sect == 1 && found == 0) {
                print key " = " val
            }
        }
    ' "$file" > "$tmp"

    cat "$tmp" > "$file"
    rm -f "$tmp"
}

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------
set_ini_kv "$SSSD_CONF" "pam" "pam_cert_auth" "True"

chown root:root "$SSSD_CONF"
chmod 0600 "$SSSD_CONF"

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------

    systemctl restart sssd
else
    systemctl start sssd || true
fi

# using authselect (the supported RHEL 8 mechanism).
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------
CURRENT_PROFILE="$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR==1 {print $NF}')" || CURRENT_PROFILE=""

if [ "$CURRENT_PROFILE" != "sssd" ]; then
    # Select sssd profile with smartcard support; backup created automatically.
    authselect select sssd with-smartcard with-smartcard-required --force
else
    # Already on sssd profile: ensure smartcard features are enabled.

        authselect enable-feature with-smartcard-required || true
    fi
fi

# Apply any pending changes to the live configuration.
authselect apply-changes >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

echo "Smart-card authentication via SSSD has been configured and integrated."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure the USBGuard service (USB device authorization
# framework) is enabled at boot and currently active on RHEL 8.
#
set -euo pipefail

SERVICE="usbguard.service"

# Determine the systemctl binary path
SYSTEMCTL="$(command -v systemctl || echo /usr/bin/systemctl)"

if [ ! -x "${SYSTEMCTL}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: systemctl not found; cannot manage services." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the usbguard package providing the service is installed.
if ! "${SYSTEMCTL}" list-unit-files "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
   ! "${SYSTEMCTL}" cat "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Installing usbguard package..."
        dnf install -y usbguard
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Installing usbguard package..."
        yum install -y usbguard
    else
        echo "ERROR: No supported package manager found to install usbguard." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Reload unit files so systemd is aware of any newly installed units.
"${SYSTEMCTL}" daemon-reload || true

# Ensure the service is enabled to start at boot (idempotent).
if ! "${SYSTEMCTL}" is-enabled "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Enabling ${SERVICE} to start at boot..."
    "${SYSTEMCTL}" enable "${SERVICE}"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already enabled at boot."
fi

# Ensure the service is currently active (idempotent).
if ! "${SYSTEMCTL}" is-active "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Starting ${SERVICE}..."
    "${SYSTEMCTL}" start "${SERVICE}"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already active."
fi

# Final verification.
if "${SYSTEMCTL}" is-enabled "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && \
   "${SYSTEMCTL}" is-active "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Ensure the USBGuard service (USB device authorization
# framework) is enabled at boot and currently active on RHEL 8.
#
set -euo pipefail

SERVICE="usbguard.service"

# Determine the systemctl binary path
SYSTEMCTL="$(command -v systemctl || echo /usr/bin/systemctl)"

if [ ! -x "${SYSTEMCTL}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: systemctl not found; cannot manage services." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the usbguard package providing the service is installed.
if ! "${SYSTEMCTL}" list-unit-files "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
   ! "${SYSTEMCTL}" cat "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Installing usbguard package..."
        dnf install -y usbguard
    elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Installing usbguard package..."
        yum install -y usbguard
    else
        echo "ERROR: No supported package manager found to install usbguard." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Reload unit files so systemd is aware of any newly installed units.
"${SYSTEMCTL}" daemon-reload || true

# Ensure the service is enabled to start at boot (idempotent).
if ! "${SYSTEMCTL}" is-enabled "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Enabling ${SERVICE} to start at boot..."
    "${SYSTEMCTL}" enable "${SERVICE}"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already enabled at boot."
fi

# Ensure the service is currently active (idempotent).
if ! "${SYSTEMCTL}" is-active "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Starting ${SERVICE}..."
    "${SYSTEMCTL}" start "${SERVICE}"
else
    echo "${SERVICE} is already active."
fi

# Final verification.
if "${SYSTEMCTL}" is-enabled "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && \
   "${SYSTEMCTL}" is-active "${SERVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "SUCCESS: ${SERVICE} is enabled and active."
    exit 0
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to ensure ${SERVICE} is enabled and active." >&2
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Idempotent remediation script to generate an initial USBGuard policy
# reflecting currently-connected USB devices, so legitimate existing
# devices remain permitted and the policy persists across reboots.
#
set -euo pipefail

POLICY_FILE="/etc/usbguard/rules.conf"
CONFIG_FILE="/etc/usbguard/usbguard-daemon.conf"

# Must run as root
if [[ "${EUID}" -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure usbguard is installed
if ! rpm -q usbguard >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Installing usbguard package..."
    yum install -y usbguard
fi

# Verify usbguard binary is available
if ! command -v usbguard >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: usbguard command not found after installation." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure configuration directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/usbguard
chmod 0755 /etc/usbguard

# Generate a policy reflecting currently connected USB devices.
# Only (re)generate if the policy file is missing or empty, to remain
# idempotent and avoid clobbering an existing, populated policy.
NEED_POLICY=0
if [[ ! -f "${POLICY_FILE}" ]]; then
    NEED_POLICY=1
elif [[ ! -s "${POLICY_FILE}" ]]; then
    NEED_POLICY=1
fi

if [[ "${NEED_POLICY}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo "Generating initial USBGuard policy from connected devices..."
    TMP_POLICY="$(mktemp)"
    # generate-policy outputs allow rules for currently attached devices
    if ! usbguard generate-policy > "${TMP_POLICY}"; then
        rm -f "${TMP_POLICY}"
        echo "ERROR: Failed to generate USBGuard policy." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -s "${TMP_POLICY}" ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Generated policy is empty; no USB devices detected." >&2
    fi

    install -o root -g root -m 0600 "${TMP_POLICY}" "${POLICY_FILE}"
    rm -f "${TMP_POLICY}"
else
    echo "Existing non-empty policy found at ${POLICY_FILE}; leaving it unchanged."
fi

# Ensure correct ownership and permissions on the policy file
chown root:root "${POLICY_FILE}"
chmod 0600 "${POLICY_FILE}"

# Ensure the daemon config points to the policy file (idempotent)
if [[ -f "${CONFIG_FILE}" ]]; then
    if grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*RuleFile[[:space:]]*=' "${CONFIG_FILE}"; then
        sed -i "s|^[[:space:]]*RuleFile[[:space:]]*=.*|RuleFile=${POLICY_FILE}|" "${CONFIG_FILE}"
    else
        echo "RuleFile=${POLICY_FILE}" >> "${CONFIG_FILE}"
    fi
    chown root:root "${CONFIG_FILE}"
    chmod 0600 "${CONFIG_FILE}"
fi

# Enable and start (or reload) the service so the policy persists across reboots
systemctl enable usbguard.service >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

if systemctl is-active --quiet usbguard.service; then
    echo "Reloading usbguard service to apply policy..."
    systemctl reload usbguard.service 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart usbguard.service
else
    echo "Starting usbguard service..."
    systemctl start usbguard.service
fi

# Confirm the service is active
if systemctl is-active --quiet usbguard.service; then
    echo "USBGuard is active and policy is in place."
else
    echo "ERROR: USBGuard service failed to start." >&2
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Remediation: Remove the X Windows graphical display server from RHEL 8.
# Ensures the host cannot boot into a graphical desktop mode (persistent).
#
set -u

LOG_TAG="remove-xwindows"

log() {
    printf '%s\n' "[$LOG_TAG] $*"
}

# Must run as root
if [[ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]]; then
    log "ERROR: This script must be run as root."
    exit 1
fi

###############################################################################
# 1. Set the default systemd target to multi-user (non-graphical), persistently
###############################################################################
if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    current_target="$(systemctl get-default 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ "$current_target" != "multi-user.target" ]]; then
        log "Setting default systemd target to multi-user.target (was: ${current_target:-unknown})."
        systemctl set-default multi-user.target >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
            log "WARNING: failed to set default target."
    else
        log "Default systemd target already multi-user.target. No change."
    fi

    # Disable/stop any graphical display manager that may be enabled.
    for dm in gdm lightdm sddm xdm kdm; do
        if systemctl list-unit-files 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${dm}\.service"; then
            if systemctl is-enabled "${dm}.service" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                log "Disabling display manager ${dm}.service."
                systemctl disable "${dm}.service" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
            fi
            if systemctl is-active "${dm}.service" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                log "Stopping display manager ${dm}.service."
                systemctl stop "${dm}.service" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
            fi
        fi
    done

    # Remove the legacy default.target symlink if it points at graphical.
    if [[ -L /etc/systemd/system/default.target ]]; then
        linkdest="$(readlink -f /etc/systemd/system/default.target 2>/dev/null || true)"
        if [[ "$linkdest" == *graphical.target ]]; then
            log "Repointing /etc/systemd/system/default.target to multi-user.target."
            ln -sf /usr/lib/systemd/system/multi-user.target \
                /etc/systemd/system/default.target
        fi
    fi
fi

###############################################################################
# 2. Remove X Windows server package groups and packages, persistently
###############################################################################
if ! command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1 && ! command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "ERROR: Neither dnf nor yum found. Cannot manage packages."
    exit 1
fi

PKG_MGR="yum"
command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1 && PKG_MGR="dnf"

# Remove environment/package groups associated with a GUI (ignore if absent).
for grp in "Server with GUI" "Workstation" "GNOME" "GNOME Desktop" \
           "KDE" "KDE Plasma Workspaces" "graphical-server-environment" \
           "X Window System" "Fonts" "base-x"; do
    if $PKG_MGR group list installed 2>/dev/null | grep -qi -- "$grp" || \
       $PKG_MGR group info "$grp" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Removing package group: ${grp}"
        $PKG_MGR -y group remove "$grp" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
done

# Explicitly remove the core X server package if present.
remove_pkg() {
    local pkg="$1"
    if rpm -q "$pkg" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "Removing package: ${pkg}"
        $PKG_MGR -y remove "$pkg" >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
            log "WARNING: failed to remove ${pkg}."
    fi
}

for pkg in xorg-x11-server-Xorg xorg-x11-server-common \
           xorg-x11-server-Xwayland xorg-x11-server-utils \
           xorg-x11-xinit; do
    remove_pkg "$pkg"
done

###############################################################################
# 3. Verify resulting state
###############################################################################
fail=0

if rpm -q xorg-x11-server-Xorg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "VERIFY FAIL: xorg-x11-server-Xorg is still installed."
    fail=1
else
    log "VERIFY OK: xorg-x11-server-Xorg is not installed."
fi

if command -v systemctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if [[ "$(systemctl get-default 2>/dev/null)" == "multi-user.target" ]]; then
        log "VERIFY OK: default target is multi-user.target."
    else
        log "VERIFY FAIL: default target is not multi-user.target."
        fail=1
    fi
fi

if [[ "$fail" -ne 0 ]]; then
    log "Remediation completed with verification failures."
    exit 1
fi

log "Remediation complete. X Windows graphical display server removed."
exit 0

    if grep -q '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"; then
        current_line=$(grep '^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=' "${GRUB_DEFAULT}")
        # Extract value between quotes
        current_val=$(echo "${current_line}" | sed -E 's/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="(.*)"$/\1/')

        # Remove any existing audit_backlog_limit entries
        new_val=$(echo "${current_val}" | sed -E "s/(^| )${PARAM}=[^ ]*//g")
        # Trim extra whitespace
        new_val=$(echo "${new_val}" | sed -E 's/^[[:space:]]+//; s/[[:space:]]+$//; s/[[:space:]]+/ /g')

        # Append the desired value
        if [ -z "${new_val}" ]; then
            new_val="${PARAM}=${VALUE}"
        else
            new_val="${new_val} ${PARAM}=${VALUE}"
        fi

        sed -i -E "s|^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${new_val}\"|" "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
    else
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${PARAM}=${VALUE}\"" >> "${GRUB_DEFAULT}"
    fi
fi

echo "Configured ${PARAM}=${VALUE} for all kernels and future installs."

# The immutable flag must be the LAST rule loaded, so we place it in a file

IMMUTABLE_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/99-finalize.rules"
AUDIT_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

if [[ ! -d "${RULES_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${RULES_DIR}"

fi

# and remains the last rule loaded.

fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/export.rules"

# Determine architecture-appropriate rules
declare -a ARCHES=("b64")
if [[ "$(uname -m)" != "aarch64" ]]; then
    ARCHES+=("b32")
fi

# Build the desired rule lines
declare -a DESIRED_RULES=()
for ARCH in "${ARCHES[@]}"; do
    DESIRED_RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=${ARCH} -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=export")
done

# Ensure the rules.d directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Remove any existing mount/export rules to avoid duplicates, then re-add
touch "$RULES_FILE"

# Strip out any prior matching rules from the file
TMP_FILE="$(mktemp)"
if [[ -f "$RULES_FILE" ]]; then
    grep -v -E '^-a[[:space:]]+always,exit.*-S[[:space:]]+mount.*-F[[:space:]]+key=export' "$RULES_FILE" > "$TMP_FILE" || true
else
    : > "$TMP_FILE"
fi

# Append the desired rules
for RULE in "${DESIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    echo "$RULE" >> "$TMP_FILE"
done

# Only update the file if it differs (idempotency)
if ! cmp -s "$TMP_FILE" "$RULES_FILE"; then
    cat "$TMP_FILE" > "$RULES_FILE"
fi
rm -f "$TMP_FILE"

chmod 0640 "$RULES_FILE"

# Load the rules so they take effect now
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
else
    for RULE in "${DESIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        # shellcheck disable=SC2086
        auditctl $RULE 2>/dev/null || true
    done
fi

exit 0

# loading via auditctl directly is effective.
if [ -f "${LEGACY_FILE}" ]; then
    if ! grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*-w[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_FILE}[[:space:]]+-p[[:space:]]+wa[[:space:]]+-k[[:space:]]+${AUDIT_KEY}[[:space:]]*$" "${LEGACY_FILE}"; then
        echo "${RULE}" >> "${LEGACY_FILE}"

fi

if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
fi

echo "Audit rule for ${AUDIT_FILE} with key '${AUDIT_KEY}' is configured."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Configure auditd to record all DAC permission-change syscalls
# (chmod, fchmod, fchmodat) performed by regular users (auid>=1000).
#

set -euo pipefail

KEY="perm_mod"
RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"

# Determine the minimum login UID for regular users.
if command -v grep >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -f /etc/login.defs ]; then
    UID_MIN=$(awk '/^[[:space:]]*UID_MIN/ {print $2; exit}' /etc/login.defs)
fi
[ -z "${UID_MIN:-}" ] && UID_MIN=1000

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists.
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Detect architecture support.
ARCH_BITS="$(getconf LONG_BIT 2>/dev/null || echo 64)"

# Build the desired rule lines.
declare -a RULES

if [ "${ARCH_BITS}" = "64" ]; then
    RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chmod,fchmod,fchmodat -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}")
fi
RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S chmod,fchmod,fchmodat -F auid>=${UID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}")

# Create the rules file if it does not exist.
touch "${RULES_FILE}"

# Idempotently ensure each rule is present.
for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -qF -- "${rule}" "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "${rule}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
    fi
done

# Load the rules into the running kernel if augenrules is available.
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        auditctl ${rule} 2>/dev/null || true
    done
fi

echo "Audit DAC permission-change rules configured in ${RULES_FILE}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Configure auditd to record successful use of ownership-changing syscalls
# (chown, fchown, lchown, fchownat) for regular users (auid>=1000),
# excluding unset login UIDs, on both b32 and b64 ABIs, tagged with key
# perm_mod. Idempotent.

set -euo pipefail

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"
KEY="perm_mod"

# Desired rules
RULES=(
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S chown -S fchown -S lchown -S fchownat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"
    "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chown -S fchown -S lchown -S fchownat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"
)

# Ensure the rules directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Create the file if it does not exist
[ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ] || touch "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Append each rule only if an equivalent one is not already present
for RULE in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    # Determine the arch of this rule for matching
    if [[ "${RULE}" == *"arch=b32"* ]]; then
        ARCH="b32"
    else
        ARCH="b64"
    fi

    # Check whether a matching rule already exists (same arch + key + ownership syscalls)
    if ! grep -Eq "arch=${ARCH}.*chown.*key=${KEY}|arch=${ARCH}.*chown.*-k[[:space:]]+${KEY}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
        echo "${RULE}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
done

# Set safe permissions
chmod 0640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Load the rules into the running audit daemon so they take effect now and
# persist across reboots (rules.d files are loaded by auditd at startup).
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" || true

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Configure auditd to record every use of the removexattr/fremovexattr/lremovexattr
# system calls, for both unprivileged users (auid>=1000) and root (auid=0),
# covering both 32-bit and 64-bit invocations, tagged with the perm_mod key.
#
set -euo pipefail

# The syscall that removes a file's extended attributes is removexattr
# (with the related fremovexattr and lremovexattr variants).

# Determine architectures to cover.
ARCHES=("b64")
# On a 64-bit system, also cover the 32-bit ABI.
if [[ "$(uname -m)" == "x86_64" || "$(uname -m)" == "aarch64" ]]; then
    ARCHES+=("b32")
fi

KEY="perm_mod"
SYSCALLS="removexattr,fremovexattr,lremovexattr"

# Build the rule lines we need.
declare -a REQUIRED_RULES=()
for arch in "${ARCHES[@]}"; do
    REQUIRED_RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${SYSCALLS} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}")
    REQUIRED_RULES+=("-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${SYSCALLS} -F auid=0 -F key=${KEY}")
done

# Normalize a rule line (collapse whitespace) for comparison purposes.
normalize() {
    # shellcheck disable=SC2001
    echo "$1" | sed -e 's/[[:space:]]\+/ /g' -e 's/^ //' -e 's/ $//' \
        -e 's/-F key=/-k /g'
}

# Check whether an equivalent rule already exists within a file.
rule_present_in_file() {
    local rule="$1" file="$2"
    [[ -f "$file" ]] || return 1
    local norm_rule
    norm_rule="$(normalize "$rule")"
    local line norm_line
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        # Skip comments and blank lines.
        [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
        [[ -z "${line// }" ]] && continue
        norm_line="$(normalize "$line")"
        if [[ "$norm_line" == "$norm_rule" ]]; then
            return 0
        fi
    done < "$file"
    return 1
}

# --- Approach 1: generated-rules approach (augenrules) ---
# Rules live in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules and are compiled into
# /etc/audit/audit.rules by augenrules.
RULES_D_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d
touch "$RULES_D_FILE"

changed=0
for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! rule_present_in_file "$rule" "$RULES_D_FILE"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$RULES_D_FILE"
        changed=1
    fi
done

# --- Approach 2: direct rule-loading approach (auditctl) ---
# Some systems load rules directly from /etc/audit/audit.rules.
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
if [[ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]]; then
    for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        if ! rule_present_in_file "$rule" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"; then
            echo "$rule" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
            changed=1
        fi
    done
fi

# --- Apply the rules to the running kernel idempotently ---
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    for rule in "${REQUIRED_RULES[@]}"; do
        # auditctl will reject exact duplicates; add only if not already loaded.
        if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q -- "key=${KEY}.*removexattr" ; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC2086
            auditctl $rule || true
        fi
    done
fi

if [[ "$changed" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo "Audit rules for removexattr (key=${KEY}) configured and loaded."
else
    echo "Audit rules for removexattr (key=${KEY}) already present; no changes made."
fi

exit 0

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
KEY="perm_mod"

mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

RULE_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"
RULE_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"

touch "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

add_rule_if_missing() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"
    # Match on the essential signature: arch and lchown and the key
    local arch
    arch=$(echo "$rule" | grep -oP 'arch=b[0-9]+')
    if ! grep -Pq -- "-S\s+lchown" "$file" 2>/dev/null || \
       ! grep -P -- "$arch" "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -Pq -- "-S\s+lchown.*key=${KEY}|key=${KEY}.*-S\s+lchown" ; then
        # More precise: check for a line containing this arch, lchown, and key together
        if ! grep -P -- "$arch" "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -P -- "-S\s+lchown" | grep -q "key=${KEY}"; then
            echo "$rule" >> "$file"
        fi
    fi
}

add_rule_if_missing "$RULE_64" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
add_rule_if_missing "$RULE_32" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Load the rules into the running configuration
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" || true
fi

exit 0
```

    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/perm_mod.rules"

else
    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
    USE_AUGENRULES=0
fi

mkdir -p "$(dirname "$RULES_FILE")"
touch "$RULES_FILE"

# Define the required rules.
RULE_64_USER="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S setxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod"
RULE_64_ROOT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S setxattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

RULE='-a always,exit -F path=/usr/bin/setfacl -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=privileged-setfacl'

# Determine the rule-loading mechanism used by auditd
if [ -f /etc/audit/auditd.conf ] && grep -iqE '^\s*flush\s*=\s*' /etc/audit/auditd.conf 2>/dev/null; then
    :
fi

# Files to write the rule into for both augenrules and auditctl mechanisms
RULES_D_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Function to ensure the rule exists in a given file (idempotent)
ensure_rule() {
    local file="$1"
    [ -f "$file" ] || touch "$file"
    # Remove any existing setfacl execution rules to avoid duplicates/conflicts
    if grep -qE '^\s*-a\s+always,exit.*path=/usr/bin/setfacl' "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        if ! grep -qxF "$RULE" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i '\#^\s*-a\s\+always,exit.*path=/usr/bin/setfacl#d' "$file"
            printf '%s\n' "$RULE" >> "$file"
        fi
    else
        printf '%s\n' "$RULE" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Configure for augenrules mechanism
ensure_rule "$RULES_D_FILE"

# Configure for auditctl mechanism
ensure_rule "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Reload the audit rules using whichever mechanism is available
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

        if ! grep -qxF "$RULE" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i '\#^\s*-a\s\+always,exit.*path=/usr/bin/setfacl#d' "$file"
            printf '%s\n' "$RULE" >> "$file"
        fi
    else
        printf '%s\n' "$RULE" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Configure for augenrules mechanism
ensure_rule "$RULES_D_FILE"

# Configure for auditctl mechanism
ensure_rule "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Reload the audit rules using whichever mechanism is available
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exit 0

RULES=(
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S creat -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S creat -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S creat -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"
"-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S creat -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=${KEY}"
)

# Determine persistent rules location
RULES_D="/etc/audit/rules.d"
MAIN_RULES="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# Decide which file to write persistent rules to.
if [ -d "${RULES_D}" ]; then
    TARGET="${RULES_D}/${KEY}.rules"
else
    TARGET="${MAIN_RULES}"
fi

# Build desired content for our dedicated rules file (only used in rules.d case)
if [ "${TARGET}" = "${RULES_D}/${KEY}.rules" ]; then
    TMP="$(mktemp)"
    for r in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        printf '%s\n' "$r" >> "${TMP}"
    done
    if [ ! -f "${TARGET}" ] || ! cmp -s "${TMP}" "${TARGET}"; then
        install -m 0640 "${TMP}" "${TARGET}"
    fi
    rm -f "${TMP}"
else
    # Append missing rules to main audit.rules idempotently
    touch "${TARGET}"
    chmod 0640 "${TARGET}"
    for r in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        if ! grep -qF -- "$r" "${TARGET}"; then
            printf '%s\n' "$r" >> "${TARGET}"
        fi
    done
fi

# Load rules into the running configuration, persisting across reboots regardless of mechanism.
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -d "${RULES_D}" ]; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v auditctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    for r in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        # Check if rule already loaded
        if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qF -- "key=${KEY}" ; then
            auditctl ${r} || true
        fi
    done
    # Ensure all rules present in running config
    for r in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        auditctl ${r} >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    done
fi

exit 0

declare -A SYSCALLS=( ["truncate"]=1 ["ftruncate"]=1 )

add_rule() {
    local arch="$1"
    local syscall="$2"
    local errno="$3"
    local rule="-a always,exit -F arch=${arch} -S ${syscall} -F exit=-${errno} -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k access"
    if ! grep -qF -- "$rule" "$RULES_FILE"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$RULES_FILE"
    fi
}

# Determine supported architectures
ARCHES=("b64")
if [ "$(uname -m)" = "x86_64" ] || [ "$(uname -m)" = "i686" ] || [ "$(uname -m)" = "i386" ]; then
    ARCHES+=("b32")
fi

for arch in "${ARCHES[@]}"; do
    for syscall in "${!SYSCALLS[@]}"; do
        add_rule "$arch" "$syscall" "EACCES"
        add_rule "$arch" "$syscall" "EPERM"
    done
done

# Load rules through the daemon's normal mechanism
if command -v augenrules >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    augenrules --load
elif command -v service >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    service auditd reload || true
fi
